```diff
@@ -1741,3 +1741,152 @@
              logger.info(f"Deleted job with UUID: {job_uuid}")
          return deleted
+ 
+ 
+ # ============ Agent Test Jobs Functions ============
+ 
+ 
+ def create_agent_test_job(
+     agent_id: str,
+     job_type: str,
+     status: str = "in_progress",
+     details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
+     results: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
+ ) -&gt; str:
+     """Create a new agent test job and return its UUID.
+ 
+     Args:
+         agent_id: UUID of the agent this job is for
+         job_type: Type of job (llm-unit-test, llm-benchmark)
+         status: Initial status (defaults to 'in_progress')
+         details: JSON config needed to re-trigger the job if interrupted
+         results: Initial results (usually None)
+     """
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         job_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())
+         details_json = json.dumps(details) if details is not None else None
+         results_json = json.dumps(results) if results is not None else None
+         cursor.execute(
+             """
+             INSERT INTO agent_test_jobs (uuid, agent_id, type, status, details, results)
+             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
+             """,
+             (job_uuid, agent_id, job_type, status, details_json, results_json),
+         )
+         conn.commit()
+         logger.info(f"Created agent test job with UUID: {job_uuid}, type: {job_type}, agent: {agent_id}")
+         return job_uuid
+ 
+ 
+ def _parse_agent_test_job_row(row: sqlite3.Row) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:
+     """Parse an agent test job database row and deserialize JSON fields."""
+     job = dict(row)
+     if job.get("details"):
+         job["details"] = json.loads(job["details"])
+     if job.get("results"):
+         job["results"] = json.loads(job["results"])
+     return job
+ 
+ 
+ def get_agent_test_job(job_uuid: str) -&gt; Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
+     """Get an agent test job by UUID."""
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM agent_test_jobs WHERE uuid = ?", (job_uuid,))
+         row = cursor.fetchone()
+         if row:
+             return _parse_agent_test_job_row(row)
+         return None
+ 
+ 
+ def get_agent_test_jobs_for_agent(
+     agent_id: str, job_type: Optional[str] = None
+ ) -&gt; List[Dict[str, Any]]:
+     """Get all agent test jobs for a specific agent, optionally filtered by type."""
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         if job_type:
+             cursor.execute(
+                 "SELECT * FROM agent_test_jobs WHERE agent_id = ? AND type = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC",
+                 (agent_id, job_type),
+             )
+         else:
+             cursor.execute(
+                 "SELECT * FROM agent_test_jobs WHERE agent_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC",
+                 (agent_id,),
+             )
+         rows = cursor.fetchall()
+         return [_parse_agent_test_job_row(row) for row in rows]
+ 
+ 
+ def get_all_agent_test_jobs(job_type: Optional[str] = None) -&gt; List[Dict[str, Any]]:
+     """Get all agent test jobs, optionally filtered by type."""
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         if job_type:
+             cursor.execute(
+                 "SELECT * FROM agent_test_jobs WHERE type = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC",
+                 (job_type,),
+             )
+         else:
+             cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM agent_test_jobs ORDER BY created_at DESC")
+         rows = cursor.fetchall()
+         return [_parse_agent_test_job_row(row) for row in rows]
+ 
+ 
+ def get_pending_agent_test_jobs() -&gt; List[Dict[str, Any]]:
+     """Get all agent test jobs with status 'in_progress' (for recovery on restart)."""
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         cursor.execute(
+             "SELECT * FROM agent_test_jobs WHERE status = 'in_progress' ORDER BY created_at ASC"
+         )
+         rows = cursor.fetchall()
+         return [_parse_agent_test_job_row(row) for row in rows]
+ 
+ 
+ def update_agent_test_job(
+     job_uuid: str,
+     status: Optional[str] = None,
+     results: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
+ ) -&gt; bool:
+     """Update an agent test job. Returns True if the job was found and updated."""
+     updates = []
+     params = []
+ 
+     if status is not None:
+         updates.append("status = ?")
+         params.append(status)
+     if results is not None:
+         updates.append("results = ?")
+         params.append(json.dumps(results))
+ 
+     if not updates:
+         return False
+ 
+     updates.append("updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP")
+     params.append(job_uuid)
+ 
+     query = f"UPDATE agent_test_jobs SET {', '.join(updates)} WHERE uuid = ?"
+ 
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         cursor.execute(query, params)
+         conn.commit()
+         updated = cursor.rowcount &gt; 0
+         if updated:
+             logger.info(f"Updated agent test job with UUID: {job_uuid}")
+         return updated
+ 
+ 
+ def delete_agent_test_job(job_uuid: str) -&gt; bool:
+     """Delete an agent test job. Returns True if the job was found and deleted."""
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         cursor.execute("DELETE FROM agent_test_jobs WHERE uuid = ?", (job_uuid,))
+         conn.commit()
+         deleted = cursor.rowcount &gt; 0
+         if deleted:
+             logger.info(f"Deleted agent test job with UUID: {job_uuid}")
+         return deleted
  
```

</details>

</tool-use>

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


## Summary of Changes

### 1. Database Schema (`db.py`)
- Created new table `agent_test_jobs` with:
  - `agent_id` column (required, foreign key to `agents` table)
  - Same structure as `jobs` table (uuid, type, status, details, results, timestamps)
  - Foreign key constraint linking to agents table

### 2. Database Functions (`db.py`)
Added functions for the new table:
- `create_agent_test_job()` - Creates a new agent test job with agent_id
- `get_agent_test_job()` - Gets a single job by UUID
- `get_agent_test_jobs_for_agent()` - Gets all jobs for a specific agent
- `get_all_agent_test_jobs()` - Gets all agent test jobs
- `get_pending_agent_test_jobs()` - Gets pending jobs for recovery
- `update_agent_test_job()` - Updates job status/results
- `delete_agent_test_job()` - Deletes a job
- `_parse_agent_test_job_row()` - Helper to parse JSON fields

### 3. Router Updates (`agent_tests.py`)
Replaced all references to generic job functions:
- `create_job()` → `create_agent_test_job()` (with `agent_id` parameter)
- `get_job()` → `get_agent_test_job()`
- `update_job()` → `update_agent_test_job()`

### Benefits
- Direct link between jobs and agents via `agent_id`
- Easier querying of jobs by agent
- Better data organization and separation of concerns
- Maintains backward compatibility with existing job structure

All changes are complete and linting passes. The agent test jobs are now stored in a dedicated table with proper agent linkage.

<details><summary>Tool use: **search_replace** • Edit file: /Users/aman/Documents/repos/artpark/pense-backend/src/db.py</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 149, lines removed: 0

```diff
@@ -1909,3 +1909,152 @@
              logger.info(f"Deleted agent test job with UUID: {job_uuid}")
          return deleted
+ 
+ 
+ # ============ Simulation Jobs Functions ============
+ 
+ 
+ def create_simulation_job(
+     simulation_id: str,
+     job_type: str,
+     status: str = "in_progress",
+     details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
+     results: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
+ ) -&gt; str:
+     """Create a new simulation job and return its UUID.
+ 
+     Args:
+         simulation_id: UUID of the simulation this job is for
+         job_type: Type of job (llm-simulation)
+         status: Initial status (defaults to 'in_progress')
+         details: JSON config needed to re-trigger the job if interrupted
+         results: Initial results (usually None)
+     """
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         job_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())
+         details_json = json.dumps(details) if details is not None else None
+         results_json = json.dumps(results) if results is not None else None
+         cursor.execute(
+             """
+             INSERT INTO simulation_jobs (uuid, simulation_id, type, status, details, results)
+             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
+             """,
+             (job_uuid, simulation_id, job_type, status, details_json, results_json),
+         )
+         conn.commit()
+         logger.info(f"Created simulation job with UUID: {job_uuid}, type: {job_type}, simulation: {simulation_id}")
+         return job_uuid
+ 
+ 
+ def _parse_simulation_job_row(row: sqlite3.Row) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:
+     """Parse a simulation job database row and deserialize JSON fields."""
+     job = dict(row)
+     if job.get("details"):
+         job["details"] = json.loads(job["details"])
+     if job.get("results"):
+         job["results"] = json.loads(job["results"])
+     return job
+ 
+ 
+ def get_simulation_job(job_uuid: str) -&gt; Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
+     """Get a simulation job by UUID."""
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM simulation_jobs WHERE uuid = ?", (job_uuid,))
+         row = cursor.fetchone()
+         if row:
+             return _parse_simulation_job_row(row)
+         return None
+ 
+ 
+ def get_simulation_jobs_for_simulation(
+     simulation_id: str, job_type: Optional[str] = None
+ ) -&gt; List[Dict[str, Any]]:
+     """Get all simulation jobs for a specific simulation, optionally filtered by type."""
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         if job_type:
+             cursor.execute(
+                 "SELECT * FROM simulation_jobs WHERE simulation_id = ? AND type = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC",
+                 (simulation_id, job_type),
+             )
+         else:
+             cursor.execute(
+                 "SELECT * FROM simulation_jobs WHERE simulation_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC",
+                 (simulation_id,),
+             )
+         rows = cursor.fetchall()
+         return [_parse_simulation_job_row(row) for row in rows]
+ 
+ 
+ def get_all_simulation_jobs(job_type: Optional[str] = None) -&gt; List[Dict[str, Any]]:
+     """Get all simulation jobs, optionally filtered by type."""
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         if job_type:
+             cursor.execute(
+                 "SELECT * FROM simulation_jobs WHERE type = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC",
+                 (job_type,),
+             )
+         else:
+             cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM simulation_jobs ORDER BY created_at DESC")
+         rows = cursor.fetchall()
+         return [_parse_simulation_job_row(row) for row in rows]
+ 
+ 
+ def get_pending_simulation_jobs() -&gt; List[Dict[str, Any]]:
+     """Get all simulation jobs with status 'in_progress' (for recovery on restart)."""
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         cursor.execute(
+             "SELECT * FROM simulation_jobs WHERE status = 'in_progress' ORDER BY created_at ASC"
+         )
+         rows = cursor.fetchall()
+         return [_parse_simulation_job_row(row) for row in rows]
+ 
+ 
+ def update_simulation_job(
+     job_uuid: str,
+     status: Optional[str] = None,
+     results: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
+ ) -&gt; bool:
+     """Update a simulation job. Returns True if the job was found and updated."""
+     updates = []
+     params = []
+ 
+     if status is not None:
+         updates.append("status = ?")
+         params.append(status)
+     if results is not None:
+         updates.append("results = ?")
+         params.append(json.dumps(results))
+ 
+     if not updates:
+         return False
+ 
+     updates.append("updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP")
+     params.append(job_uuid)
+ 
+     query = f"UPDATE simulation_jobs SET {', '.join(updates)} WHERE uuid = ?"
+ 
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         cursor.execute(query, params)
+         conn.commit()
+         updated = cursor.rowcount &gt; 0
+         if updated:
+             logger.info(f"Updated simulation job with UUID: {job_uuid}")
+         return updated
+ 
+ 
+ def delete_simulation_job(job_uuid: str) -&gt; bool:
+     """Delete a simulation job. Returns True if the job was found and deleted."""
+     with get_db_connection() as conn:
+         cursor = conn.cursor()
+         cursor.execute("DELETE FROM simulation_jobs WHERE uuid = ?", (job_uuid,))
+         conn.commit()
+         deleted = cursor.rowcount &gt; 0
+         if deleted:
+             logger.info(f"Deleted simulation job with UUID: {job_uuid}")
+         return deleted
  
```

</details>

</tool-use>

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


All changes are complete. Summary:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. Database Schema (`db.py`)
- Created new table `simulation_jobs` with:
  - `simulation_id` column (required, foreign key to `simulations` table)
  - Same structure as `jobs` table (uuid, type, status, details, results, timestamps)
  - Foreign key constraint linking to simulations table

### 2. Database Functions (`db.py`)
Added functions for the new table:
- `create_simulation_job()` - Creates a new simulation job with simulation_id
- `get_simulation_job()` - Gets a single job by UUID
- `get_simulation_jobs_for_simulation()` - Gets all jobs for a specific simulation
- `get_all_simulation_jobs()` - Gets all simulation jobs
- `get_pending_simulation_jobs()` - Gets pending jobs for recovery
- `update_simulation_job()` - Updates job status/results
- `delete_simulation_job()` - Deletes a job
- `_parse_simulation_job_row()` - Helper to parse JSON fields

### 3. Router Updates (`simulations.py`)
Replaced all references to generic job functions:
- `create_job()` → `create_simulation_job()` (with `simulation_id` parameter)
- `get_job()` → `get_simulation_job()`
- `update_job()` → `update_simulation_job()`

### Benefits
- Direct link between jobs and simulations via `simulation_id`
- Easier querying of jobs by simulation
- Better data organization and separation of concerns
- Maintains backward compatibility with existing job structure

All changes are complete and linting passes. Simulation jobs are now stored in a dedicated table with proper simulation linkage.

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Removed the three DELETE endpoints:

1. `DELETE /{simulation_uuid}/personas/{persona_uuid}` - Remove persona from simulation
2. `DELETE /{simulation_uuid}/scenarios/{scenario_uuid}` - Remove scenario from simulation  
3. `DELETE /{simulation_uuid}/metrics/{metric_uuid}` - Remove metric from simulation

The POST endpoints for adding personas/scenarios/metrics remain, and the PUT endpoint can still replace them. The DELETE endpoints for individual removal have been removed.

Linting passes. Changes complete.

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


## Summary of Changes

### 1. **Removed parameters from `RunSimulationRequest`**
   - Removed `agent_uuid` (optional)
   - Removed `model` (optional)
   - Now an empty model — no parameters needed

### 2. **Updated `_build_pense_simulation_config`**
   - Removed `model` parameter
   - Always extracts model from agent's LLM config: `agent_config.get("llm", {}).get("model", "gpt-4.1")`
   - Matches the pattern in `agent_tests.py`

### 3. **Updated `run_simulation_task`**
   - Removed `model` parameter
   - Gets model from the pense config built from agent

### 4. **Updated `run_simulation_endpoint`**
   - Always uses `simulation.get("agent_id")` — no fallback to request
   - Requires agent to be linked to simulation (returns 400 if not)
   - Removed model from job details
   - Updated thread args to not pass model

### Behavior
- Uses the agent linked to the simulation (`simulation.agent_id`)
- Uses the model from the agent's LLM config (`agent.config.llm.model`)
- No request parameters needed — just `POST /simulations/{simulation_uuid}/run` with empty body

This matches the pattern used in `agent_tests.py` where the model is always derived from the agent's configuration.

---

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Removed the three POST endpoints for adding individual personas, scenarios, and metrics to simulations:

1. **Removed endpoints:**
   - `POST /{simulation_uuid}/personas` - Add persona to simulation
   - `POST /{simulation_uuid}/scenarios` - Add scenario to simulation
   - `POST /{simulation_uuid}/metrics` - Add metric to simulation

2. **Removed request models:**
   - `AddPersonaRequest`
   - `AddScenarioRequest`
   - `AddMetricRequest`

3. **Cleaned up unused imports:**
   - Removed `get_simulation_persona_link`, `get_simulation_scenario_link`, `get_simulation_metric_link`

**What remains:**
- The `PUT /{simulation_uuid}` endpoint can still replace all personas/scenarios/metrics at once
- The `POST /{simulation_uuid}` endpoint (create simulation) can still link them during creation

Linting passes. Changes complete.

+         return v

Adding the GET endpoint for listing runs:

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="search_replace">

Lines added: 27, lines removed: 0

```diff
@@ -283,4 +283,31 @@
      )
  
+ 
+ @router.get("/{simulation_uuid}/runs", response_model=SimulationRunsResponse)
+ async def get_simulation_runs(simulation_uuid: str):
+     """
+     Get all runs for a simulation.
+ 
+     Returns a list of all simulation runs with their UUID, status, and type.
+     """
+     # Verify simulation exists
+     simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)
+     if not simulation:
+         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Simulation not found")
+ 
+     # Get all jobs for this simulation
+     jobs = get_simulation_jobs_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)
+     
+     runs = [
+         SimulationRunListItem(
+             uuid=job["uuid"],
+             status=job["status"],
+             type=job["type"],
+         )
+         for job in jobs
+     ]
+ 
+     return SimulationRunsResponse(runs=runs)
+ 
  
  @router.get("/{simulation_uuid}", response_model=SimulationDetailResponse)
```

</details>

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


## Summary of Changes

### 1. **Updated `RunSimulationRequest` Model**
   - Added `type` field (required) that accepts either `"chat"` or `"voice"`
   - Added Pydantic field validator to ensure only valid types are accepted

### 2. **Updated Run Endpoint**
   - Changed `POST /{simulation_uuid}/run` to accept `type` in the request body
   - Updated job creation to use `request.type` instead of hardcoded `"chat"`

### 3. **Added New Response Models**
   - `SimulationRunListItem` - Contains uuid, status, and type for a single run
   - `SimulationRunsResponse` - Contains a list of runs

### 4. **Added New GET Endpoint**
   - `GET /{simulation_uuid}/runs` - Returns all runs for a simulation
   - Returns: list of runs with uuid, status, and type
   - Verifies simulation exists before returning results

### 5. **Updated Imports**
   - Added `get_simulation_jobs_for_simulation` to imports
   - Added `Field` and `field_validator` from pydantic

### API Usage Examples

**Run a simulation:**
```json
POST /simulations/{simulation_uuid}/run
{
  "type": "chat"  // or "voice"
}
```

**Get all runs for a simulation:**
```
GET /simulations/{simulation_uuid}/runs
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "runs": [
    {
      "uuid": "job-uuid-1",
      "status": "done",
      "type": "chat"
    },
    {
      "uuid": "job-uuid-2",
      "status": "in_progress",
      "type": "voice"
    }
  ]
}
```

All changes are complete and linting passes.

+             try:
+                 if job_type == "stt-eval":
+                     _recover_stt_job(job_id, details)
+                 elif job_type == "tts-eval":
+                     _recover_tts_job(job_id, details)
+                 else:
+                     logger.warning(f"Unknown job type: {job_type}, marking as failed")
+                     update_job(
+                         job_id,
+                         status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,
+                         results={
+                             "error": f"Job recovery failed: unknown job type {job_type}"
+                         },
+                     )
+             except Exception as e:
+                 logger.error(f"Failed to recover job {job_id}: {e}")
+                 update_job(
+                     job_id,
+                     status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,
+                     results={"error": f"Job recovery failed: {str(e)}"},
+                 )
+     else:
+         logger.info("No in_progress generic jobs to recover")
+ 
+     # Recover agent test jobs
+     pending_agent_test_jobs = get_pending_agent_test_jobs()
+     if pending_agent_test_jobs:
+         logger.info(
+             f"Found {len(pending_agent_test_jobs)} in_progress agent test job(s) to recover"
+         )
+         for job in pending_agent_test_jobs:
+             job_id = job["uuid"]
+             job_type = job["type"]
+             details = job.get("details")
+ 
+             if not details:
+                 logger.warning(f"Agent test job {job_id} has no details, marking as failed")
+                 update_agent_test_job(
+                     job_id,
+                     status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,
+                     results={"error": "Job recovery failed: no details available"},
+                 )
+                 continue
+ 
+             try:
+                 if job_type == "llm-unit-test":
+                     _recover_llm_unit_test_job(job_id, details)
+                 elif job_type == "llm-benchmark":
+                     _recover_llm_benchmark_job(job_id, details)
+                 else:
+                     logger.warning(
+                         f"Unknown agent test job type: {job_type}, marking as failed"
+                     )
+                     update_agent_test_job(
+                         job_id,
+                         status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,
+                         results={
+                             "error": f"Job recovery failed: unknown job type {job_type}"
+                         },
+                     )
+             except Exception as e:
+                 logger.error(f"Failed to recover agent test job {job_id}: {e}")
+                 update_agent_test_job(
+                     job_id,
+                     status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,
+                     results={"error": f"Job recovery failed: {str(e)}"},
+                 )
+     else:
+         logger.info("No in_progress agent test jobs to recover")
+ 
+     # Recover simulation jobs
+     pending_simulation_jobs = get_pending_simulation_jobs()
+     if pending_simulation_jobs:
+         logger.info(
+             f"Found {len(pending_simulation_jobs)} in_progress simulation job(s) to recover"
+         )
+         for job in pending_simulation_jobs:
+             job_id = job["uuid"]
+             job_type = job["type"]
+             details = job.get("details")
+ 
+             if not details:
+                 logger.warning(
+                     f"Simulation job {job_id} has no details, marking as failed"
+                 )
+                 update_simulation_job(
+                     job_id,
+                     status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,
+                     results={"error": "Job recovery failed: no details available"},
+                 )
+                 continue
+ 
+             try:
+                 if job_type in ["chat", "voice"]:
+                     _recover_simulation_job(job_id, details, job_type)
+                 else:
+                     logger.warning(
+                         f"Unknown simulation job type: {job_type}, marking as failed"
+                     )
+                     update_simulation_job(
+                         job_id,
+                         status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,
+                         results={
+                             "error": f"Job recovery failed: unknown job type {job_type}"
+                         },
+                     )
+             except Exception as e:
+                 logger.error(f"Failed to recover simulation job {job_id}: {e}")
+                 update_simulation_job(
+                     job_id,
+                     status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,
+                     results={"error": f"Job recovery failed: {str(e)}"},
+                 )
+     else:
+         logger.info("No in_progress simulation jobs to recover")

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="search_replace">

Lines added: 39, lines removed: 0

```diff
@@ -269,3 +269,42 @@
      thread.start()
      logger.info(f"LLM benchmark job {job_id} recovery started")
+ 
+ 
+ def _recover_simulation_job(job_id: str, details: dict, job_type: str):
+     """Recover a simulation job (chat or voice)."""
+     from routers.simulations import run_simulation_task
+ 
+     logger.info(f"Recovering simulation job {job_id} (type: {job_type})")
+ 
+     simulation_uuid = details["simulation_uuid"]
+     agent_uuid = details["agent_uuid"]
+     s3_bucket = details["s3_bucket"]
+ 
+     # Verify simulation exists
+     simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)
+     if not simulation:
+         raise ValueError(f"Simulation {simulation_uuid} not found")
+ 
+     # Fetch agent
+     agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)
+     if not agent:
+         raise ValueError(f"Agent {agent_uuid} not found")
+ 
+     # Fetch personas, scenarios, and metrics
+     personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)
+     scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)
+     metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)
+ 
+     if not personas:
+         raise ValueError(f"Simulation {simulation_uuid} has no personas")
+     if not scenarios:
+         raise ValueError(f"Simulation {simulation_uuid} has no scenarios")
+ 
+     thread = threading.Thread(
+         target=run_simulation_task,
+         args=(job_id, agent, personas, scenarios, metrics, s3_bucket),
+         daemon=True,
+     )
+     thread.start()
+     logger.info(f"Simulation job {job_id} recovery started")
  
```

</details>

</tool-use>

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


## Summary of Changes

### 1. **Updated Imports (`job_recovery.py`)**
   - Added `get_pending_agent_test_jobs` and `get_pending_simulation_jobs`
   - Added `update_agent_test_job` and `update_simulation_job`
   - Added simulation-related functions: `get_simulation`, `get_personas_for_simulation`, `get_scenarios_for_simulation`, `get_metrics_for_simulation`

### 2. **Refactored `recover_pending_jobs()` Function**
   - Split into three sections:
     - **Generic jobs** (stt-eval, tts-eval) — uses `update_job`
     - **Agent test jobs** (llm-unit-test, llm-benchmark) — uses `update_agent_test_job`
     - **Simulation jobs** (chat, voice) — uses `update_simulation_job`
   - Each section handles its own error cases and logging

### 3. **Added `_recover_simulation_job()` Function**
   - Recovers simulation jobs (both chat and voice types)
   - Fetches simulation, agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics from database
   - Validates that simulation has required entities
   - Restarts the simulation task in a background thread

### 4. **Updated Existing Recovery Functions**
   - `_recover_llm_unit_test_job` and `_recover_llm_benchmark_job` remain unchanged (they already work correctly)

### Recovery Flow

On app startup (`main.py`):
1. Initializes database
2. Calls `recover_pending_jobs()` which:
   - Recovers generic jobs (stt-eval, tts-eval)
   - Recovers agent test jobs (llm-unit-test, llm-benchmark)
   - Recovers simulation jobs (chat, voice)

All three job types are now automatically recovered on application restart. The `main.py` file already calls `recover_pending_jobs()` correctly, so no changes were needed there.

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Added the `updated_at` field to the runs API response.

## Changes Made

### 1. **Updated `SimulationRunListItem` Model**
   - Added `updated_at: str` field

### 2. **Updated `get_simulation_runs` Endpoint**
   - Includes `updated_at` from the job when creating `SimulationRunListItem` objects

### API Response Example

Now the `/simulations/{simulation_uuid}/runs` endpoint returns:

```json
{
  "runs": [
    {
      "uuid": "job-uuid-1",
      "status": "done",
      "type": "chat",
      "updated_at": "2024-01-15 10:30:45"
    },
    {
      "uuid": "job-uuid-2",
      "status": "in_progress",
      "type": "voice",
      "updated_at": "2024-01-15 11:15:20"
    }
  ]
}
```

All changes are complete and linting passes.

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Added `type` and `updated_at` to the simulation run status API response.

## Changes Made

### 1. **Updated `SimulationRunStatusResponse` Model**
   - Added `type: str` - The run type (chat or voice)
   - Added `updated_at: str` - Last update timestamp

### 2. **Updated `get_simulation_run_status` Endpoint**
   - Includes `type` and `updated_at` from the job when creating the response

### API Response Example

Now the `GET /simulations/run/{task_id}` endpoint returns:

```json
{
  "task_id": "job-uuid-123",
  "status": "done",
  "type": "chat",
  "updated_at": "2024-01-15 10:30:45",
  "total_simulations": 4,
  "metrics": {...},
  "simulation_results": [...],
  "results_s3_prefix": "simulations/runs/job-uuid-123",
  "error": null
}
```

All changes are complete and linting passes.

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Updated the simulation task to use a persistent output directory instead of a temporary one.

## Changes Made

### 1. **Removed Temporary Directory**
   - Removed `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()` context manager
   - Removed unused `tempfile` import

### 2. **Created Persistent Directory Structure**
   - Creates a persistent directory at `simulation_outputs/{task_id}/`
   - The directory structure is: `simulation_outputs/{task_id}/output/`
   - Directory persists after job completion

### Directory Structure

After a simulation run completes, the output will be stored at:
```
simulation_outputs/
  └── {task_id}/
      └── output/
          ├── simulation_config.json
          ├── metrics.json
          ├── results.csv
          ├── simulation_persona_1_scenario_1/
          │   ├── transcript.json
          │   ├── evaluation_results.csv
          │   └── logs/
          └── ...
```

The directory will remain on disk after the job completes, allowing you to inspect the results locally. Files are still uploaded to S3 as before, but the local directory is preserved.

All changes are complete and linting passes.

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Fixed the path issue. Summary of changes:

## Fixes Applied

### 1. **Resolved Output Directory Paths**
   - Resolved `output_dir` to an absolute path at the start of `_run_pense_simulation`
   - Resolved `output_dir` in `run_simulation_task` before passing it to the function
   - Ensures all paths are absolute when passed to the pense command

### 2. **Path Resolution Flow**
   - `run_simulation_task`: Creates `output_dir = temp_path / "output"` and resolves it to absolute
   - `_run_pense_simulation`: Receives the path and resolves it again (defensive) to ensure absolute paths
   - Config file path is now absolute: `{absolute_path}/simulation_config.json`
   - Output directory path is now absolute: `{absolute_path}`

### Why This Fixes the Error

The error showed pense was trying to read `simulation_outputs/a161d5cc-2683-46d5-962f-60447c4be3bc/output/simulation_config.json` as a relative path. By resolving paths to absolute before passing them to pense, the command can locate the config file regardless of the working directory.

The simulation should now run correctly with the persistent output directory structure.

---

        "contents": "\n\n\ndef create_agent(name: str, config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -\u003e str:\n    \"\"\"Create a new agent and return its UUID.\"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        # Generate UUID for the agent\n        agent_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n        # Serialize config to JSON string for storage\n        config_json = json.dumps(config) if config is not None else None\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO agents (uuid, name, config)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (agent_uuid, name, config_json),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(f\"Created agent with UUID: {agent_uuid}\")\n        return agent_uuid",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef create_agent(name: str, config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -\u003e str:\n    \"\"\"Create a new agent and return its UUID.\"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        # Generate UUID for the agent\n        agent_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n        # Serialize config to JSON string for storage\n        config_json = json.dumps(config) if config is not None else None\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO agents (uuid, name, config)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (agent_uuid, name, config_json),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(f\"Created agent with UUID: {agent_uuid}\")\n        return agent_uuid",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef init_db():\n    \"\"\"Initialize the database and create tables if they don't exist.\"\"\"\n    # Ensure the data directory exists\n    DB_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agents (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                description TEXT,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                agent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                tool_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL,\n                UNIQUE(agent_id, tool_id),\n                FOREIGN KEY (agent_id) REFERENCES agents(uuid),\n                FOREIGN KEY (tool_id) REFERENCES tools(uuid)\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )",

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef init_db():\n    \"\"\"Initialize the database and create tables if they don't exist.\"\"\"\n    # Ensure the data directory exists\n    DB_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agents (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                description TEXT,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                agent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                tool_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL,\n                UNIQUE(agent_id, tool_id),\n                FOREIGN KEY (agent_id) REFERENCES agents(uuid),\n                FOREIGN KEY (tool_id) REFERENCES tools(uuid)\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 297
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 19
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/db.py"
      },

        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"\", response_model=AgentCreateResponse)\nasync def create_agent_endpoint(agent: AgentCreate):\n    \"\"\"Create a new agent.\"\"\"\n    agent_uuid = create_agent(\n        name=agent.name,\n        config=agent.config,\n    )\n    return AgentCreateResponse(uuid=agent_uuid, message=\"Agent created successfully\")",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Run Simulation API ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense simulation config from agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config containing system_prompt and llm.model\n        personas: List of persona dicts with description and config (containing gender, language)\n        scenarios: List of scenario dicts with description\n        metrics: List of metric dicts with name and description (for evaluation_criteria)\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from agent config\n    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Build personas list as objects with characteristics, gender, and language\n    persona_list = []\n    for persona in personas:\n        persona_config = persona.get(\"config\") or {}\n        persona_obj = {\n            \"characteristics\": persona.get(\"description\") or persona.get(\"name\"),\n            \"gender\": persona_config.get(\"gender\", \"female\"),\n            \"language\": persona_config.get(\"language\", \"english\"),\n        }\n        if persona_obj[\"characteristics\"]:\n            persona_list.append(persona_obj)\n\n    # Build scenarios list (use description field)\n    scenario_list = [\n        scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n        for scenario in scenarios\n        if scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n    ]",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 475,
            "text": "@router.delete(\"/{simulation_uuid}\")"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 476,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 480
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 481,
            "text": "    return {\"message\": \"Simulation deleted successfully\"}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 482

            "text": "    # Get model from agent config"

            "text": "        for tool in agent_tools"

            "lineNumber": 517,
            "text": "    ]"

            "lineNumber": 518
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 519,
            "text": "    # Build personas list as objects with characteristics, gender, and language"

          },

            "lineNumber": 521,
            "text": "    for persona in personas:"

            "lineNumber": 522,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 523,
            "text": "        persona_obj = {"
          },

            "lineNumber": 524,
            "text": "            \"characteristics\": persona.get(\"description\") or persona.get(\"name\"),"
          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 526,
            "text": "            \"language\": persona_config.get(\"language\", \"english\"),"
          },

            "lineNumber": 527,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 528,
            "text": "        if persona_obj[\"characteristics\"]:"
          },

            "lineNumber": 529,
            "text": "            persona_list.append(persona_obj)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 530
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 531,
            "text": "    # Build scenarios list (use description field)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 532,
            "text": "    scenario_list = ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 533,
            "text": "        scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 534,
            "text": "        for scenario in scenarios"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 535,
            "text": "        if scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 536,

            "lineNumber": 537
          },

            "lineNumber": 538,
            "text": "    # Build evaluation criteria from metrics"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Run Simulation API ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense simulation config from agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config containing system_prompt and llm.model\n        personas: List of persona dicts with description and config (containing gender, language)\n        scenarios: List of scenario dicts with description\n        metrics: List of metric dicts with name and description (for evaluation_criteria)\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from agent config\n    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Build personas list as objects with characteristics, gender, and language\n    persona_list = []\n    for persona in personas:\n        persona_config = persona.get(\"config\") or {}\n        persona_obj = {\n            \"characteristics\": persona.get(\"description\") or persona.get(\"name\"),\n            \"gender\": persona_config.get(\"gender\", \"female\"),\n            \"language\": persona_config.get(\"language\", \"english\"),\n        }\n        if persona_obj[\"characteristics\"]:\n            persona_list.append(persona_obj)\n\n    # Build scenarios list (use description field)\n    scenario_list = [\n        scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n        for scenario in scenarios\n        if scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n    ]",
        "range": {

          "startPosition": {
            "line": 474
          }

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "&#96;logs&#96; contains the full logs of the simulation including all the pipecat logs whereas &#96;results.log&#96; contains only the terminal output of the simulation as shown in the sample output above.\n\nYou can checkout [&#96;pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output&#96;](pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output) to see a sample output of the simulation.\n\n## Voice agent simulations\n\nTo run agent simulations with all the three components - STT, LLM, and TTS - prepare a JSON configuration file as shown below.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n\\nYou always speak in english.\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\nOnce all the questions have been answered, end the call by calling the &#96;end_call&#96; tool immediately without asking or saying anything else to the user.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience)\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive true/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked. Only when all the questions have been asked and answered, then, call the &#96;end_call&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\n\", // prompt for the voice agent\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 767,
            "text": "    \"std\": 0.0,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 768,
            "text": "    \"values\": [1.0, 1.0, 1.0]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 769,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 770,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 771,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 772
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 773,
            "text": "&#96;logs&#96; contains the full logs of the simulation including all the pipecat logs whereas &#96;results.log&#96; contains only the terminal output of the simulation as shown in the sample output above."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 774
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 775,
            "text": "You can checkout [&#96;pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output&#96;](pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output) to see a sample output of the simulation."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 776
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 777,
            "text": "## Voice agent simulations"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 778
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 779,
            "text": "To run agent simulations with all the three components - STT, LLM, and TTS - prepare a JSON configuration file as shown below."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 780
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 781,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 782,
            "text": "{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 783,
            "text": "  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n\\nYou always speak in english.\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\nOnce all the questions have been answered, end the call by calling the &#96;end_call&#96; tool immediately without asking or saying anything else to the user.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience)\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive true/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked. Only when all the questions have been asked and answered, then, call the &#96;end_call&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\n\", // prompt for the voice agent"
          },

            "text": "  \"language\": \"english\", // language of the conversation"

If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22.     {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef update_agent(\n    agent_uuid: str,\n    name: Optional[str] = None,\n    config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n) -\u003e bool:\n    \"\"\"Update an agent. Returns True if the agent was found and updated.\"\"\"\n    # Build dynamic update query\n    updates = []\n    params = []\n\n    if name is not None:\n        updates.append(\"name = ?\")\n        params.append(name)\n    if config is not None:\n        updates.append(\"config = ?\")\n        # Serialize config to JSON string for storage\n        params.append(json.dumps(config))\n\n    if not updates:\n        return False\n\n    updates.append(\"updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\")\n    params.append(agent_uuid)\n\n    query = (\n        f\"UPDATE agents SET {', '.join(updates)} WHERE uuid = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL\"\n    )\n\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(query, params)\n        conn.commit()\n        updated = cursor.rowcount \u003e 0\n        if updated:\n            logger.info(f\"Updated agent with UUID: {agent_uuid}\")\n        return updated",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 344,
            "text": "def get_all_agents() -\u003e List[Dict[str, Any]]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 351
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef update_agent(\n    agent_uuid: str,\n    name: Optional[str] = None,\n    config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n) -\u003e bool:\n    \"\"\"Update an agent. Returns True if the agent was found and updated.\"\"\"\n    # Build dynamic update query\n    updates = []\n    params = []\n\n    if name is not None:\n        updates.append(\"name = ?\")\n        params.append(name)\n    if config is not None:\n        updates.append(\"config = ?\")\n        # Serialize config to JSON string for storage\n        params.append(json.dumps(config))\n\n    if not updates:\n        return False\n\n    updates.append(\"updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\")\n    params.append(agent_uuid)\n\n    query = (\n        f\"UPDATE agents SET {', '.join(updates)} WHERE uuid = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL\"\n    )\n\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(query, params)\n        conn.commit()\n        updated = cursor.rowcount \u003e 0\n        if updated:\n            logger.info(f\"Updated agent with UUID: {agent_uuid}\")\n        return updated",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 418
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 343
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/db.py"
      },
      "score": 0.28073906898498535
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "{\n  \"params\": {\n    \"model\": \"google/gemini-3-flash-preview\"\n  },\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n4: Age (number)\\n5: Previous stillbirth or neonatal loss?\\n6: History of 3 or more consecutive spontaneous abortions\\n7: Birth weight of last baby (string)\\n8: Last pregnancy: hospital admission for hypertension or pre-eclampsia/eclampsia?\\n9: Previous surgery on reproductive tract (Caesarean section, myomectomy, cone biopsy, cervical cerclage,)\\n10: Diagnosed or suspected multiple pregnancy\\n11: Isoimmunisation Rh (-) in current or previous pregnancy\\n12: Vaginal bleeding\\n13: Pelvic mass\\n14: Diastolic blood pressure 90 mmHg or more at booking\\n15: Diabetes mellitus on insulin or oral hypoglycaemic treatment\\n16: Cardiac disease\\n17: Renal disease\\n18: Epilepsy\\n19: Asthmatic on medication\\n20: Tuberculosis\\n21: Known \\u2018substance\\u2019 abuse (including heavy alcohol drinking)\\n22: Any other severe medical disease or condition (string)\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience, if they have given this answer across multiple messages consider that valid too - don't force the user to repeat the number in one go if all the 10 digits are already clear from the conversation)\\nAge: must be a valid age value\\nBirth weight of last baby: must be a valid weight value with the appropriate units (except if this is their first baby, in which case this should be null\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nThe user may have given the answer to a question in parts across several messages. For example, they might have clarified something in a later message or corrected something they missed earlier. So, make sure to look for the complete answer in the user's response across all the messages and don't unnecessarily repeat the same question to the user as long as the full answer is clear from the entire conversation history so far.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive True/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\nWhenever you invoke the &#96;fill_form&#96; tool, always use Roman characters for the string values\",\n",
        "detailedLines": [

            "text": "{"
          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 3,

If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22.         "originalContents": "{\n  \"params\": {\n    \"model\": \"google/gemini-3-flash-preview\"\n  },\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n4: Age (number)\\n5: Previous stillbirth or neonatal loss?\\n6: History of 3 or more consecutive spontaneous abortions\\n7: Birth weight of last baby (string)\\n8: Last pregnancy: hospital admission for hypertension or pre-eclampsia/eclampsia?\\n9: Previous surgery on reproductive tract (Caesarean section, myomectomy, cone biopsy, cervical cerclage,)\\n10: Diagnosed or suspected multiple pregnancy\\n11: Isoimmunisation Rh (-) in current or previous pregnancy\\n12: Vaginal bleeding\\n13: Pelvic mass\\n14: Diastolic blood pressure 90 mmHg or more at booking\\n15: Diabetes mellitus on insulin or oral hypoglycaemic treatment\\n16: Cardiac disease\\n17: Renal disease\\n18: Epilepsy\\n19: Asthmatic on medication\\n20: Tuberculosis\\n21: Known \\u2018substance\\u2019 abuse (including heavy alcohol drinking)\\n22: Any other severe medical disease or condition (string)\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user.     {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Agent Test Jobs Functions ============\n\n\ndef create_agent_test_job(\n    agent_id: str,\n    job_type: str,\n    status: str = \"in_progress\",\n    details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n    results: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n) -\u003e str:\n    \"\"\"Create a new agent test job and return its UUID.\n\n    Args:\n        agent_id: UUID of the agent this job is for\n        job_type: Type of job (llm-unit-test, llm-benchmark)\n        status: Initial status (defaults to 'in_progress')\n        details: JSON config needed to re-trigger the job if interrupted\n        results: Initial results (usually None)\n    \"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        job_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n        details_json = json.dumps(details) if details is not None else None\n        results_json = json.dumps(results) if results is not None else None\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO agent_test_jobs (uuid, agent_id, type, status, details, results)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (job_uuid, agent_id, job_type, status, details_json, results_json),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Created agent test job with UUID: {job_uuid}, type: {job_type}, agent: {agent_id}\"\n        )\n        return job_uuid",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 1750,
            "text": "def delete_job(job_uuid: str) -\u003e bool:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1758
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 1759,
            "text": "        return deleted"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1760
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1761
          },

            "lineNumber": 1762,

            "lineNumber": 1763

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Agent Test Jobs Functions ============\n\n\ndef create_agent_test_job(\n    agent_id: str,\n    job_type: str,\n    status: str = \"in_progress\",\n    details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n    results: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n) -\u003e str:\n    \"\"\"Create a new agent test job and return its UUID.\n\n    Args:\n        agent_id: UUID of the agent this job is for\n        job_type: Type of job (llm-unit-test, llm-benchmark)\n        status: Initial status (defaults to 'in_progress')\n        details: JSON config needed to re-trigger the job if interrupted\n        results: Initial results (usually None)\n    \"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        job_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n        details_json = json.dumps(details) if details is not None else None\n        results_json = json.dumps(results) if results is not None else None\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO agent_test_jobs (uuid, agent_id, type, status, details, results)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (job_uuid, agent_id, job_type, status, details_json, results_json),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Created agent test job with UUID: {job_uuid}, type: {job_type}, agent: {agent_id}\"\n        )\n        return job_uuid",

        "contents": "\n\n\ndef get_agents_for_tool(tool_id: str) -\u003e List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"Get all agents associated with a tool.\"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT a.* FROM agents a\n            INNER JOIN agent_tools at ON a.uuid = at.agent_id\n            WHERE at.tool_id = ? AND at.deleted_at IS NULL AND a.deleted_at IS NULL\n            ORDER BY at.created_at DESC\n            \"\"\",\n            (tool_id,),\n        )\n        rows = cursor.fetchall()\n        return [_parse_agent_row(row) for row in rows]",

AND at.deleted_at IS NULL AND a.deleted_at IS NULL\n            ORDER BY at.created_at DESC\n            \"\"\",\n            (test_id,),\n        )\n        rows = cursor.fetchall()\n        return [_parse_agent_row(row) for row in rows]",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef add_tool_to_agent(agent_id: str, tool_id: str) -\u003e int:\n    \"\"\"Add a tool to an agent. Returns the id of the created/restored link.\n    If a soft-deleted link exists, it will be restored by unsetting deleted_at.\n    \"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        # Check if a soft-deleted link exists\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"SELECT id FROM agent_tools WHERE agent_id = ? AND tool_id = ? AND deleted_at IS NOT NULL\",\n            (agent_id, tool_id),\n        )\n        existing = cursor.fetchone()\n        if existing:\n            # Restore the soft-deleted link\n            cursor.execute(\n                \"UPDATE agent_tools SET deleted_at = NULL WHERE id = ?\",\n                (existing[\"id\"],),\n            )\n            conn.commit()\n            logger.info(f\"Restored tool {tool_id} to agent {agent_id}\")\n            return existing[\"id\"]\n\n        # Insert new link\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO agent_tools (agent_id, tool_id)\n            VALUES (?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (agent_id, tool_id),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        link_id = cursor.lastrowid\n        logger.info(f\"Added tool {tool_id} to agent {agent_id}\")\n        return link_id",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef add_tool_to_agent(agent_id: str, tool_id: str) -\u003e int:\n    \"\"\"Add a tool to an agent. Returns the id of the created/restored link.\n    If a soft-deleted link exists, it will be restored by unsetting deleted_at.\n    \"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        # Check if a soft-deleted link exists\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"SELECT id FROM agent_tools WHERE agent_id = ? AND tool_id = ? AND deleted_at IS NOT NULL\",\n            (agent_id, tool_id),\n        )\n        existing = cursor.fetchone()\n        if existing:\n            # Restore the soft-deleted link\n            cursor.execute(\n                \"UPDATE agent_tools SET deleted_at = NULL WHERE id = ?\",\n                (existing[\"id\"],),\n            )\n            conn.commit()\n            logger.info(f\"Restored tool {tool_id} to agent {agent_id}\")\n            return existing[\"id\"]\n\n        # Insert new link\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO agent_tools (agent_id, tool_id)\n            VALUES (?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (agent_id, tool_id),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        link_id = cursor.lastrowid\n        logger.info(f\"Added tool {tool_id} to agent {agent_id}\")\n        return link_id",
