    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"\", response_model=AgentToolsCreateResponse)\nasync def create_agent_tool_links(agent_tools: AgentToolsCreate):\n    \"\"\"Add tools to an agent.\"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_tools.agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Verify all tools exist\n    for tool_uuid in agent_tools.tool_uuids:\n        tool = get_tool(tool_uuid)\n        if not tool:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Tool {tool_uuid} not found\")\n\n    link_ids = []\n    for tool_uuid in agent_tools.tool_uuids:\n        # Check if link already exists\n        existing = get_agent_tool_link(agent_tools.agent_uuid, tool_uuid)\n        if existing:\n            continue  # Skip already linked tools\n\n        try:\n            link_id = add_tool_to_agent(\n                agent_id=agent_tools.agent_uuid,\n                tool_id=tool_uuid,\n            )\n            link_ids.append(link_id)\n        except IntegrityError:\n            continue  # Skip if already linked\n\n    return AgentToolsCreateResponse(\n        ids=link_ids, message=\"Tools added to agent successfully\"\n    )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"\", response_model=AgentToolsCreateResponse)\nasync def create_agent_tool_links(agent_tools: AgentToolsCreate):\n    \"\"\"Add tools to an agent.\"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_tools.agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Verify all tools exist\n    for tool_uuid in agent_tools.tool_uuids:\n        tool = get_tool(tool_uuid)\n        if not tool:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Tool {tool_uuid} not found\")\n\n    link_ids = []\n    for tool_uuid in agent_tools.tool_uuids:\n        # Check if link already exists\n        existing = get_agent_tool_link(agent_tools.agent_uuid, tool_uuid)\n        if existing:\n            continue  # Skip already linked tools\n\n        try:\n            link_id = add_tool_to_agent(\n                agent_id=agent_tools.agent_uuid,\n                tool_id=tool_uuid,\n            )\n            link_ids.append(link_id)\n        except IntegrityError:\n            continue  # Skip if already linked\n\n    return AgentToolsCreateResponse(\n        ids=link_ids, message=\"Tools added to agent successfully\"\n    )",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 98
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 51
          }

        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/agent_tools.py"

      "score": 0.24588890373706818
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/agent/{agent_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_agent_test(agent_uuid: str, request: RunTestRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run one or more tests for an agent.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM tests command\n    with the agent's config and the combined test cases from all specified tests.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    if not request.test_uuids:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400, detail=\"At least one test UUID is required\"\n        )\n\n    # Verify all tests exist and are linked to the agent\n    tests = []\n    for test_uuid in request.test_uuids:\n        test = get_test(test_uuid)\n        if not test:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} not found\")\n\n        # Verify agent-test link exists\n        link = get_agent_test_link(agent_uuid, test_uuid)\n        if not link:\n            raise HTTPException(\n                status_code=400,\n                detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} is not linked to this agent. Link the test first.\",\n            )\n\n        tests.append(test)\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_agent_test_job(\n        agent_id=agent_uuid,\n        job_type=\"llm-unit-test\",\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"agent_uuid\": agent_uuid,\n            \"test_uuids\": request.test_uuids,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )\n\n    # Start background task in a separate thread\n    thread = threading.Thread(\n        target=run_llm_test_task,\n        args=(job_id, agent, tests, s3_bucket),\n        daemon=True,\n    )\n    thread.start()\n\n    return TaskCreateResponse(task_id=job_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 391,
            "text": "def run_llm_test_task("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 392,
            "text": "    task_id: str,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 393,
            "text": "    agent: Dict[str, Any],"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 394,
            "text": "    tests: List[Dict[str, Any]],"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 395,
            "text": "    s3_bucket: str,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 396,
            "text": "):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 456
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 457,
            "text": "            results={\"error\": f\"Task failed: {str(e)}\"},"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 458,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 459
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 460
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 461,
            "text": "@router.post(\"/agent/{agent_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 462,
            "text": "async def run_agent_test(agent_uuid: str, request: RunTestRequest):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 463,

          {
            "lineNumber": 528,
            "text": "@router.get(\"/run/{task_id}\", response_model=TestRunStatusResponse)"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 529,
            "text": "async def get_agent_test_run_status(task_id: str):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 530,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/agent/{agent_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_agent_test(agent_uuid: str, request: RunTestRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run one or more tests for an agent.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM tests command\n    with the agent's config and the combined test cases from all specified tests.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    if not request.test_uuids:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400, detail=\"At least one test UUID is required\"\n        )\n\n    # Verify all tests exist and are linked to the agent\n    tests = []\n    for test_uuid in request.test_uuids:\n        test = get_test(test_uuid)\n        if not test:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} not found\")\n\n        # Verify agent-test link exists\n        link = get_agent_test_link(agent_uuid, test_uuid)\n        if not link:\n            raise HTTPException(\n                status_code=400,\n                detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} is not linked to this agent. Link the test first.\",\n            )\n\n        tests.append(test)\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_agent_test_job(\n        agent_id=agent_uuid,\n        job_type=\"llm-unit-test\",\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"agent_uuid\": agent_uuid,\n            \"test_uuids\": request.test_uuids,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )\n\n    # Start background task in a separate thread\n    thread = threading.Thread(\n        target=run_llm_test_task,\n        args=(job_id, agent, tests, s3_bucket),\n        daemon=True,\n    )\n    thread.start()\n\n    return TaskCreateResponse(task_id=job_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 549
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 390
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/agent_tests.py"
      },

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef get_tools_for_agent(agent_id: str) -\u003e List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"Get all tools associated with an agent.\"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT t.* FROM tools t\n            INNER JOIN agent_tools at ON t.uuid = at.tool_id\n            WHERE at.agent_id = ? AND at.deleted_at IS NULL AND t.deleted_at IS NULL\n            ORDER BY at.created_at DESC\n            \"\"\",\n            (agent_id,),\n        )\n        rows = cursor.fetchall()\n        return [_parse_tool_row(row) for row in rows]",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef get_tools_for_agent(agent_id: str) -\u003e List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"Get all tools associated with an agent.\"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT t.* FROM tools t\n            INNER JOIN agent_tools at ON t.uuid = at.tool_id\n            WHERE at.agent_id = ? AND at.deleted_at IS NULL AND t.deleted_at IS NULL\n            ORDER BY at.created_at DESC\n            \"\"\",\n            (agent_id,),\n        )\n        rows = cursor.fetchall()\n        return [_parse_tool_row(row) for row in rows]",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Agent Tests Functions ============\n\n\ndef add_test_to_agent(agent_id: str, test_id: str) -\u003e int:\n    \"\"\"Add a test to an agent. Returns the id of the created/restored link.\n    If a soft-deleted link exists, it will be restored by unsetting deleted_at.\n    \"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        # Check if a soft-deleted link exists\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"SELECT id FROM agent_tests WHERE agent_id = ? AND test_id = ? AND deleted_at IS NOT NULL\",\n            (agent_id, test_id),\n        )\n        existing = cursor.fetchone()\n        if existing:\n            # Restore the soft-deleted link\n            cursor.execute(\n                \"UPDATE agent_tests SET deleted_at = NULL WHERE id = ?\",\n                (existing[\"id\"],),\n            )\n            conn.commit()\n            logger.info(f\"Restored test {test_id} to agent {agent_id}\")\n            return existing[\"id\"]\n        else:\n            # Insert new link\n            cursor.execute(\n                \"\"\"\n                INSERT INTO agent_tests (agent_id, test_id)\n                VALUES (?, ?)\n                \"\"\",\n                (agent_id, test_id),\n            )\n            conn.commit()\n            link_id = cursor.lastrowid\n            logger.info(f\"Added test {test_id} to agent {agent_id}\")\n            return link_id",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,

          },

            "lineNumber": 1516,

          },

            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 1517,
            "text": "        return [dict(row) for row in rows]"

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Agent Tests Functions ============\n\n\ndef add_test_to_agent(agent_id: str, test_id: str) -\u003e int:\n    \"\"\"Add a test to an agent. Returns the id of the created/restored link.\n    If a soft-deleted link exists, it will be restored by unsetting deleted_at.\n    \"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        # Check if a soft-deleted link exists\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"SELECT id FROM agent_tests WHERE agent_id = ? AND test_id = ? AND deleted_at IS NOT NULL\",\n            (agent_id, test_id),\n        )\n        existing = cursor.fetchone()\n        if existing:\n            # Restore the soft-deleted link\n            cursor.execute(\n                \"UPDATE agent_tests SET deleted_at = NULL WHERE id = ?\",\n                (existing[\"id\"],),\n            )\n            conn.commit()\n            logger.info(f\"Restored test {test_id} to agent {agent_id}\")\n            return existing[\"id\"]\n        else:\n            # Insert new link\n            cursor.execute(\n                \"\"\"\n                INSERT INTO agent_tests (agent_id, test_id)\n                VALUES (?, ?)\n                \"\"\",\n                (agent_id, test_id),\n            )\n            conn.commit()\n            link_id = cursor.lastrowid\n            logger.info(f\"Added test {test_id} to agent {agent_id}\")\n            return link_id",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"\", response_model=AgentTestsCreateResponse)\nasync def create_agent_test_links(agent_tests: AgentTestsCreate):\n    \"\"\"Add tests to an agent.\"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_tests.agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Verify all tests exist\n    for test_uuid in agent_tests.test_uuids:\n        test = get_test(test_uuid)\n        if not test:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} not found\")\n\n    link_ids = []\n    for test_uuid in agent_tests.test_uuids:\n        # Check if link already exists\n        existing = get_agent_test_link(agent_tests.agent_uuid, test_uuid)\n        if existing:\n            continue  # Skip already linked tests\n\n        try:\n            link_id = add_test_to_agent(\n                agent_id=agent_tests.agent_uuid,\n                test_id=test_uuid,\n            )\n            link_ids.append(link_id)\n        except IntegrityError:\n            continue  # Skip if already linked\n\n    return AgentTestsCreateResponse(\n        ids=link_ids, message=\"Tests added to agent successfully\"\n    )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "text": "class TestRunStatusResponse(BaseModel):"

            "lineNumber": 109
          },

            "text": "    results_s3_prefix: Optional[str] = None"

            "text": "            link_id = add_test_to_agent("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137,
            "text": "                agent_id=agent_tests.agent_uuid,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138,
            "text": "                test_id=test_uuid,"
          },
          {

          },

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "@router.get(\"\", response_model=List[AgentTestResponse])"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "async def list_agent_tests():"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "    \"\"\"List all agent-test links.\"\"\""
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"\", response_model=AgentTestsCreateResponse)\nasync def create_agent_test_links(agent_tests: AgentTestsCreate):\n    \"\"\"Add tests to an agent.\"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_tests.agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Verify all tests exist\n    for test_uuid in agent_tests.test_uuids:\n        test = get_test(test_uuid)\n        if not test:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} not found\")\n\n    link_ids = []\n    for test_uuid in agent_tests.test_uuids:\n        # Check if link already exists\n        existing = get_agent_test_link(agent_tests.agent_uuid, test_uuid)\n        if existing:\n            continue  # Skip already linked tests\n\n        try:\n            link_id = add_test_to_agent(\n                agent_id=agent_tests.agent_uuid,\n                test_id=test_uuid,\n            )\n            link_ids.append(link_id)\n        except IntegrityError:\n            continue  # Skip if already linked\n\n    return AgentTestsCreateResponse(\n        ids=link_ids, message=\"Tests added to agent successfully\"\n    )",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 152
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 102
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/agent_tests.py"
      },
      "score": 0.22086119651794434
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef init_db():\n    \"\"\"Initialize the database and create tables if they don't exist.\"\"\"\n    # Ensure the data directory exists\n    DB_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agents (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                description TEXT,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                agent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                tool_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL,\n                UNIQUE(agent_id, tool_id),\n                FOREIGN KEY (agent_id) REFERENCES agents(uuid),\n                FOREIGN KEY (tool_id) REFERENCES tools(uuid)\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "@contextmanager"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "def get_db_connection():"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    try:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "        yield conn"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    finally:"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "        conn.close()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef init_db():\n    \"\"\"Initialize the database and create tables if they don't exist.\"\"\"\n    # Ensure the data directory exists\n    DB_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agents (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                description TEXT,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                agent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                tool_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL,\n                UNIQUE(agent_id, tool_id),\n                FOREIGN KEY (agent_id) REFERENCES agents(uuid),\n                FOREIGN KEY (tool_id) REFERENCES tools(uuid)\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )",
        "range": {

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.get(\"/tool/{tool_uuid}/agents\", response_model=List[AgentResponse])\nasync def get_tool_agents(tool_uuid: str):\n    \"\"\"Get all agents for a specific tool.\"\"\"\n    # Verify tool exists\n    tool = get_tool(tool_uuid)\n    if not tool:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Tool not found\")\n\n    agents = get_agents_for_tool(tool_uuid)\n    return agents",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.get(\"/tool/{tool_uuid}/agents\", response_model=List[AgentResponse])\nasync def get_tool_agents(tool_uuid: str):\n    \"\"\"Get all agents for a specific tool.\"\"\"\n    # Verify tool exists\n    tool = get_tool(tool_uuid)\n    if not tool:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Tool not found\")\n\n    agents = get_agents_for_tool(tool_uuid)\n    return agents",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/agent/{agent_uuid}/benchmark\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_agent_benchmark(agent_uuid: str, request: BenchmarkRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a benchmark comparing multiple models on the same tests.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM tests command\n    for each model in parallel, then generates a leaderboard comparing results.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    if not request.test_uuids:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400, detail=\"At least one test UUID is required\"\n        )\n\n    if not request.models:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=\"At least one model is required\")\n\n    # Verify all tests exist and are linked to the agent\n    tests = []\n    for test_uuid in request.test_uuids:\n        test = get_test(test_uuid)\n        if not test:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} not found\")\n\n        link = get_agent_test_link(agent_uuid, test_uuid)\n        if not link:\n            raise HTTPException(\n                status_code=400,\n                detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} is not linked to this agent. Link the test first.\",\n            )\n\n        tests.append(test)\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_agent_test_job(\n        agent_id=agent_uuid,\n        job_type=\"llm-benchmark\",\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"agent_uuid\": agent_uuid,\n            \"test_uuids\": request.test_uuids,\n            \"models\": request.models,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 632,
            "text": "def run_benchmark_task("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 633,
            "text": "    task_id: str,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 634,
            "text": "    agent: Dict[str, Any],"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 635,
            "text": "    tests: List[Dict[str, Any]],"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 636,
            "text": "    models: List[str],"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 637,
            "text": "    s3_bucket: str,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 638,
            "text": "):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 789
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 790,
            "text": "            results={\"error\": f\"Task failed: {str(e)}\"},"
          },

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/agent/{agent_uuid}/benchmark\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_agent_benchmark(agent_uuid: str, request: BenchmarkRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a benchmark comparing multiple models on the same tests.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM tests command\n    for each model in parallel, then generates a leaderboard comparing results.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    if not request.test_uuids:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400, detail=\"At least one test UUID is required\"\n        )\n\n    if not request.models:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=\"At least one model is required\")\n\n    # Verify all tests exist and are linked to the agent\n    tests = []\n    for test_uuid in request.test_uuids:\n        test = get_test(test_uuid)\n        if not test:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} not found\")\n\n        link = get_agent_test_link(agent_uuid, test_uuid)\n        if not link:\n            raise HTTPException(\n                status_code=400,\n                detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} is not linked to this agent. Link the test first.\",\n            )\n\n        tests.append(test)\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_agent_test_job(\n        agent_id=agent_uuid,\n        job_type=\"llm-benchmark\",\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"agent_uuid\": agent_uuid,\n            \"test_uuids\": request.test_uuids,\n            \"models\": request.models,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 860
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 631
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/agent_tests.py"
      },

    },

        "contents": "\n\n\ndef get_agent_tool_link(agent_id: str, tool_id: str) -\u003e Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"Check if a specific agent-tool link exists.\"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"SELECT * FROM agent_tools WHERE agent_id = ? AND tool_id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL\",\n            (agent_id, tool_id),\n        )\n        row = cursor.fetchone()\n        if row:\n            return dict(row)\n        return None",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{simulation_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a simulation with personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM simulations command\n    with the agent's config and the simulation's personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Uses the agent linked to the simulation and its LLM model configuration.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Simulation not found\")\n\n    # Get agent from simulation\n    agent_uuid = simulation.get(\"agent_id\")\n    if not agent_uuid:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"No agent linked to this simulation. Link an agent to the simulation first.\",\n        )\n\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Get linked entities\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no personas. Add at least one persona.\",\n        )\n\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no scenarios. Add at least one scenario.\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{simulation_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a simulation with personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM simulations command\n    with the agent's config and the simulation's personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Uses the agent linked to the simulation and its LLM model configuration.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Simulation not found\")\n\n    # Get agent from simulation\n    agent_uuid = simulation.get(\"agent_id\")\n    if not agent_uuid:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"No agent linked to this simulation. Link an agent to the simulation first.\",\n        )\n\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Get linked entities\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no personas. Add at least one persona.\",\n        )\n\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no scenarios. Add at least one scenario.\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Run Simulation API ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense simulation config from agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config containing system_prompt and llm.model\n        personas: List of persona dicts with description and config (containing gender, language)\n        scenarios: List of scenario dicts with description\n        metrics: List of metric dicts with name and description (for evaluation_criteria)\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from agent config\n    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Build personas list as objects with characteristics, gender, and language\n    persona_list = []\n    for persona in personas:\n        persona_config = persona.get(\"config\") or {}\n        persona_obj = {\n            \"characteristics\": persona.get(\"description\") or persona.get(\"name\"),\n            \"gender\": persona_config.get(\"gender\", \"female\"),\n            \"language\": persona_config.get(\"language\", \"english\"),\n        }\n        if persona_obj[\"characteristics\"]:\n            persona_list.append(persona_obj)\n\n    # Build scenarios list (use description field)\n    scenario_list = [\n        scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n        for scenario in scenarios\n        if scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n    ]",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 475,

          },

            "lineNumber": 480
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 481,
            "text": "    return {\"message\": \"Simulation deleted successfully\"}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 482
          },

            "lineNumber": 483
          },

          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Run Simulation API ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense simulation config from agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config containing system_prompt and llm.model\n        personas: List of persona dicts with description and config (containing gender, language)\n        scenarios: List of scenario dicts with description\n        metrics: List of metric dicts with name and description (for evaluation_criteria)\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from agent config\n    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Build personas list as objects with characteristics, gender, and language\n    persona_list = []\n    for persona in personas:\n        persona_config = persona.get(\"config\") or {}\n        persona_obj = {\n            \"characteristics\": persona.get(\"description\") or persona.get(\"name\"),\n            \"gender\": persona_config.get(\"gender\", \"female\"),\n            \"language\": persona_config.get(\"language\", \"english\"),\n        }\n        if persona_obj[\"characteristics\"]:\n            persona_list.append(persona_obj)\n\n    # Build scenarios list (use description field)\n    scenario_list = [\n        scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n        for scenario in scenarios\n        if scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n    ]",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 554
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 474
          }

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Shared Helper Functions ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    tests: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    model: Optional[str] = None,\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense test config from agent and tests.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config\n        tests: List of test dicts with config\n        model: Optional model override. If None, uses agent's llm.model or defaults to gpt-4.1\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from param or agent config\n    if model is None:\n        llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n        model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Combine test cases from all tests\n    all_test_cases = []\n    for test in tests:\n        test_config = test.get(\"config\")\n        if not test_config:\n            continue\n\n        if test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"type\"] == \"tool_call\":\n            tool_calls = []\n            for tool_call in test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"]:\n                tool_calls.append(\n                    {\n                        \"tool\": tool_call[\"tool\"],\n                        \"arguments\": (\n                            tool_call[\"arguments\"]\n                            if not tool_call.get(\"accept_any_arguments\", False)\n                            else None\n                        ),\n                    }\n                )\n            test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"] = tool_calls\n\n        all_test_cases.append(test_config)\n\n    return {\n        \"params\": {\"model\": model},\n        \"system_prompt\": agent_config.get(\"system_prompt\", \"\"),\n        \"tools\": tool_configs,\n        \"test_cases\": all_test_cases,\n    }",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Shared Helper Functions ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    tests: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    model: Optional[str] = None,\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense test config from agent and tests.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config\n        tests: List of test dicts with config\n        model: Optional model override. If None, uses agent's llm.model or defaults to gpt-4.1\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from param or agent config\n    if model is None:\n        llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n        model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Combine test cases from all tests\n    all_test_cases = []\n    for test in tests:\n        test_config = test.get(\"config\")\n        if not test_config:\n            continue\n\n        if test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"type\"] == \"tool_call\":\n            tool_calls = []\n            for tool_call in test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"]:\n                tool_calls.append(\n                    {\n                        \"tool\": tool_call[\"tool\"],\n                        \"arguments\": (\n                            tool_call[\"arguments\"]\n                            if not tool_call.get(\"accept_any_arguments\", False)\n                            else None\n                        ),\n                    }\n                )\n            test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"] = tool_calls\n\n        all_test_cases.append(test_config)\n\n    return {\n        \"params\": {\"model\": model},\n        \"system_prompt\": agent_config.get(\"system_prompt\", \"\"),\n        \"tools\": tool_configs,\n        \"test_cases\": all_test_cases,\n    }",

{
  "codeResults": [
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "{\n  \"params\": {\n    \"model\": \"google/gemini-3-flash-preview\"\n  },\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n4: Age (number)\\n5: Previous stillbirth or neonatal loss?\\n6: History of 3 or more consecutive spontaneous abortions\\n7: Birth weight of last baby (string)\\n8: Last pregnancy: hospital admission for hypertension or pre-eclampsia/eclampsia?\\n9: Previous surgery on reproductive tract (Caesarean section, myomectomy, cone biopsy, cervical cerclage,)\\n10: Diagnosed or suspected multiple pregnancy\\n11: Isoimmunisation Rh (-) in current or previous pregnancy\\n12: Vaginal bleeding\\n13: Pelvic mass\\n14: Diastolic blood pressure 90 mmHg or more at booking\\n15: Diabetes mellitus on insulin or oral hypoglycaemic treatment\\n16: Cardiac disease\\n17: Renal disease\\n18: Epilepsy\\n19: Asthmatic on medication\\n20: Tuberculosis\\n21: Known \\u2018substance\\u2019 abuse (including heavy alcohol drinking)\\n22: Any other severe medical disease or condition (string)\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience, if they have given this answer across multiple messages consider that valid too - don't force the user to repeat the number in one go if all the 10 digits are already clear from the conversation)\\nAge: must be a valid age value\\nBirth weight of last baby: must be a valid weight value with the appropriate units (except if this is their first baby, in which case this should be null\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nThe user may have given the answer to a question in parts across several messages. For example, they might have clarified something in a later message or corrected something they missed earlier. So, make sure to look for the complete answer in the user's response across all the messages and don't unnecessarily repeat the same question to the user as long as the full answer is clear from the entire conversation history so far.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive True/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\nWhenever you invoke the &#96;fill_form&#96; tool, always use Roman characters for the string values\",\n",
        "detailedLines": [

            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "{"
          },

          },

            "text": "  },"

            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n4: Age (number)\\n5: Previous stillbirth or neonatal loss?\\n6: History of 3 or more consecutive spontaneous abortions\\n7: Birth weight of last baby (string)\\n8: Last pregnancy: hospital admission for hypertension or pre-eclampsia/eclampsia?\\n9: Previous surgery on reproductive tract (Caesarean section, myomectomy, cone biopsy, cervical cerclage,)\\n10: Diagnosed or suspected multiple pregnancy\\n11: Isoimmunisation Rh (-) in current or previous pregnancy\\n12: Vaginal bleeding\\n13: Pelvic mass\\n14: Diastolic blood pressure 90 mmHg or more at booking\\n15: Diabetes mellitus on insulin or oral hypoglycaemic treatment\\n16: Cardiac disease\\n17: Renal disease\\n18: Epilepsy\\n19: Asthmatic on medication\\n20: Tuberculosis\\n21: Known \\u2018substance\\u2019 abuse (including heavy alcohol drinking)\\n22: Any other severe medical disease or condition (string)\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience, if they have given this answer across multiple messages consider that valid too - don't force the user to repeat the number in one go if all the 10 digits are already clear from the conversation)\\nAge: must be a valid age value\\nBirth weight of last baby: must be a valid weight value with the appropriate units (except if this is their first baby, in which case this should be null\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nThe user may have given the answer to a question in parts across several messages. For example, they might have clarified something in a later message or corrected something they missed earlier. So, make sure to look for the complete answer in the user's response across all the messages and don't unnecessarily repeat the same question to the user as long as the full answer is clear from the entire conversation history so far.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive True/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\nWhenever you invoke the &#96;fill_form&#96; tool, always use Roman characters for the string values\","

        "originalContents": "{\n  \"params\": {\n    \"model\": \"google/gemini-3-flash-preview\"\n  },\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n4: Age (number)\\n5: Previous stillbirth or neonatal loss?\\n6: History of 3 or more consecutive spontaneous abortions\\n7: Birth weight of last baby (string)\\n8: Last pregnancy: hospital admission for hypertension or pre-eclampsia/eclampsia?\\n9: Previous surgery on reproductive tract (Caesarean section, myomectomy, cone biopsy, cervical cerclage,)\\n10: Diagnosed or suspected multiple pregnancy\\n11: Isoimmunisation Rh (-) in current or previous pregnancy\\n12: Vaginal bleeding\\n13: Pelvic mass\\n14: Diastolic blood pressure 90 mmHg or more at booking\\n15: Diabetes mellitus on insulin or oral hypoglycaemic treatment\\n16: Cardiac disease\\n17: Renal disease\\n18: Epilepsy\\n19: Asthmatic on medication\\n20: Tuberculosis\\n21: Known \\u2018substance\\u2019 abuse (including heavy alcohol drinking)\\n22: Any other severe medical disease or condition (string)\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience, if they have given this answer across multiple messages consider that valid too - don't force the user to repeat the number in one go if all the 10 digits are already clear from the conversation)\\nAge: must be a valid age value\\nBirth weight of last baby: must be a valid weight value with the appropriate units (except if this is their first baby, in which case this should be null\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nThe user may have given the answer to a question in parts across several messages. For example, they might have clarified something in a later message or corrected something they missed earlier. So, make sure to look for the complete answer in the user's response across all the messages and don't unnecessarily repeat the same question to the user as long as the full answer is clear from the entire conversation history so far.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive True/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\nWhenever you invoke the &#96;fill_form&#96; tool, always use Roman characters for the string values\",\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "&#96;logs&#96; contains the full logs of the simulation including all the pipecat logs whereas &#96;results.log&#96; contains only the terminal output of the simulation as shown in the sample output above.\n\nYou can checkout [&#96;pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output&#96;](pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output) to see a sample output of the simulation.\n\n## Voice agent simulations\n\nTo run agent simulations with all the three components - STT, LLM, and TTS - prepare a JSON configuration file as shown below.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n\\nYou always speak in english.\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\nOnce all the questions have been answered, end the call by calling the &#96;end_call&#96; tool immediately without asking or saying anything else to the user.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience)\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive true/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked. Only when all the questions have been asked and answered, then, call the &#96;end_call&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\n\", // prompt for the voice agent\n",
        "detailedLines": [

            "lineNumber": 767,

          {
            "lineNumber": 777,
            "text": "## Voice agent simulations"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 778
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 779,
            "text": "To run agent simulations with all the three components - STT, LLM, and TTS - prepare a JSON configuration file as shown below."

            "lineNumber": 781,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;json"
