```json
            "lineNumber": 782,
            "text": "{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 783,
            "text": "  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n\\nYou always speak in english.\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\nOnce all the questions have been answered, end the call by calling the &#96;end_call&#96; tool immediately without asking or saying anything else to the user.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience)\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive true/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked. Only when all the questions have been asked and answered, then, call the &#96;end_call&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\n\", // prompt for the voice agent"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 784,
            "text": "  \"language\": \"english\", // language of the conversation"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "&#96;logs&#96; contains the full logs of the simulation including all the pipecat logs whereas &#96;results.log&#96; contains only the terminal output of the simulation as shown in the sample output above.\n\nYou can checkout [&#96;pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output&#96;](pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output) to see a sample output of the simulation.\n\n## Voice agent simulations\n\nTo run agent simulations with all the three components - STT, LLM, and TTS - prepare a JSON configuration file as shown below.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n\\nYou always speak in english.\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\nOnce all the questions have been answered, end the call by calling the &#96;end_call&#96; tool immediately without asking or saying anything else to the user.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience)\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive true/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked. Only when all the questions have been asked and answered, then, call the &#96;end_call&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\n\", // prompt for the voice agent\n",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 784
          },
          "startPosition": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Shared Helper Functions ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    tests: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    model: Optional[str] = None,\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense test config from agent and tests.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config\n        tests: List of test dicts with config\n        model: Optional model override. If None, uses agent's llm.model or defaults to gpt-4.1\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from param or agent config\n    if model is None:\n        llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n        model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Combine test cases from all tests\n    all_test_cases = []\n    for test in tests:\n        test_config = test.get(\"config\")\n        if not test_config:\n            continue\n\n        if test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"type\"] == \"tool_call\":\n            tool_calls = []\n            for tool_call in test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"]:\n                tool_calls.append(\n                    {\n                        \"tool\": tool_call[\"tool\"],\n                        \"arguments\": (\n                            tool_call[\"arguments\"]\n                            if not tool_call.get(\"accept_any_arguments\", False)\n                            else None\n                        ),\n                    }\n                )\n            test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"] = tool_calls\n\n        all_test_cases.append(test_config)\n\n    return {\n        \"params\": {\"model\": model},\n        \"system_prompt\": agent_config.get(\"system_prompt\", \"\"),\n        \"tools\": tool_configs,\n        \"test_cases\": all_test_cases,\n    }",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Shared Helper Functions ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    tests: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    model: Optional[str] = None,\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense test config from agent and tests.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config\n        tests: List of test dicts with config\n        model: Optional model override. If None, uses agent's llm.model or defaults to gpt-4.1\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from param or agent config\n    if model is None:\n        llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n        model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Combine test cases from all tests\n    all_test_cases = []\n    for test in tests:\n        test_config = test.get(\"config\")\n        if not test_config:\n            continue\n\n        if test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"type\"] == \"tool_call\":\n            tool_calls = []\n            for tool_call in test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"]:\n                tool_calls.append(\n                    {\n                        \"tool\": tool_call[\"tool\"],\n                        \"arguments\": (\n                            tool_call[\"arguments\"]\n                            if not tool_call.get(\"accept_any_arguments\", False)\n                            else None\n                        ),\n                    }\n                )\n            test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"] = tool_calls\n\n        all_test_cases.append(test_config)\n\n    return {\n        \"params\": {\"model\": model},\n        \"system_prompt\": agent_config.get(\"system_prompt\", \"\"),\n        \"tools\": tool_configs,\n        \"test_cases\": all_test_cases,\n    }",
        "range": {

            "line": 179

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "2. Pick or edit a config file. &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/config.json&#96; is a ready-to-use config file.\n   You can configure the providers for STT, LLM, and TTS in the config file as shown below:\n\n   **Supported Providers:**\n\n   - **STT**: deepgram, google, openai, elevenlabs, sarvam, cartesia, smallest\n   - **TTS**: elevenlabs, cartesia, google, openai, smallest, deepgram, sarvam\n   - **LLM**: openrouter, openai\n\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;json\n   {\n     \"system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful assistant.\",\n     \"language\": \"english\",\n     \"stt\": {\n       \"provider\": \"deepgram\"\n     },\n     \"tts\": {\n       \"provider\": \"cartesia\",\n       \"voice_id\": \"YOUR_VOICE_ID\"\n     },\n     \"llm\": {\n       \"provider\": \"openrouter\", // or \"openai\"\n       \"model\": \"openai/gpt-4o-2024-11-20\" // or \"gpt-4.1\"\n     },\n     \"tools\": [\n       {\n         \"type\": \"client\",\n         \"name\": \"get_weather\",\n         \"description\": \"Get the current weather\",\n         \"parameters\": [\n           {\n             \"id\": \"location\",\n             \"type\": \"string\",\n             \"description\": \"The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA\",\n             \"required\": true\n           }\n         ]\n       }\n     ]\n   }\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n3. Start the runner:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense agent test -c pense/agent/examples/test/config.json -o ./out/run\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOnce you run it, you can open &#96;http://localhost:7860/client/&#96; in your browser. Click **Connect**, and begin talking to the agent through your browser.\n\n\u003e **⚠️ Important**  \n\u003e Avoid using your laptop's speaker and microphone at the same time. Audio from the speaker can be picked up by the microphone, interrupting the agent with its own voice.  \n\u003e **Use a headphone with a built-in microphone.**\n\nThe client UI streams the conversation transcript in real time, while the **Metrics** tab mirrors the live latency statistics reported by the pipeline as shown in the images below:\n\n![Conversation example](images/run_conversation.png)\n\n![Metrics example](images/run_metrics.png)\n\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "1. Make sure your &#96;.env&#96; file has the required API keys (defaults use OpenAI STT + LLM and Google TTS)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "2. Pick or edit a config file. &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/config.json&#96; is a ready-to-use config file."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "   You can configure the providers for STT, LLM, and TTS in the config file as shown below:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "   **Supported Providers:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "   - **STT**: deepgram, google, openai, elevenlabs, sarvam, cartesia, smallest"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "   - **TTS**: elevenlabs, cartesia, google, openai, smallest, deepgram, sarvam"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "   - **LLM**: openrouter, openai"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "   {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "     \"system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful assistant.\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "     \"language\": \"english\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "     \"stt\": {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "       \"provider\": \"deepgram\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "     },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "     \"tts\": {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "       \"provider\": \"cartesia\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "       \"voice_id\": \"YOUR_VOICE_ID\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "     },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "     \"llm\": {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "       \"provider\": \"openrouter\", // or \"openai\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "       \"model\": \"openai/gpt-4o-2024-11-20\" // or \"gpt-4.1\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "     },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "     \"tools\": ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "       {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "         \"type\": \"client\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "         \"name\": \"get_weather\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "         \"description\": \"Get the current weather\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "         \"parameters\": ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "           {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "             \"id\": \"location\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "             \"type\": \"string\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "             \"description\": \"The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "             \"required\": true"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "           }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "         ]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "       }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "     ]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "   }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "3. Start the runner:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "pense agent test -c pense/agent/examples/test/config.json -o ./out/run"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "Once you run it, you can open &#96;http://localhost:7860/client/&#96; in your browser. Click **Connect**, and begin talking to the agent through your browser."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "\u003e **⚠️ Important**  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "\u003e Avoid using your laptop's speaker and microphone at the same time. Audio from the speaker can be picked up by the microphone, interrupting the agent with its own voice.  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "\u003e **Use a headphone with a built-in microphone.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "The client UI streams the conversation transcript in real time, while the **Metrics** tab mirrors the live latency statistics reported by the pipeline as shown in the images below:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "![Conversation example](images/run_conversation.png)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "![Metrics example](images/run_metrics.png)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "Every user/bot audio turn is persisted inside &#96;\u003coutput_dir\u003e/audios&#96; (see a sample output folder for the exact layout). The terminal also logs each transcript message and every function/tool call issued by the LLM so you can audit the interaction without leaving your shell."
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "2. Pick or edit a config file. &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/config.json&#96; is a ready-to-use config file.\n   You can configure the providers for STT, LLM, and TTS in the config file as shown below:\n\n   **Supported Providers:**\n\n   - **STT**: deepgram, google, openai, elevenlabs, sarvam, cartesia, smallest\n   - **TTS**: elevenlabs, cartesia, google, openai, smallest, deepgram, sarvam\n   - **LLM**: openrouter, openai\n\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;json\n   {\n     \"system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful assistant.\",\n     \"language\": \"english\",\n     \"stt\": {\n       \"provider\": \"deepgram\"\n     },\n     \"tts\": {\n       \"provider\": \"cartesia\",\n       \"voice_id\": \"YOUR_VOICE_ID\"\n     },\n     \"llm\": {\n       \"provider\": \"openrouter\", // or \"openai\"\n       \"model\": \"openai/gpt-4o-2024-11-20\" // or \"gpt-4.1\"\n     },\n     \"tools\": [\n       {\n         \"type\": \"client\",\n         \"name\": \"get_weather\",\n         \"description\": \"Get the current weather\",\n         \"parameters\": [\n           {\n             \"id\": \"location\",\n             \"type\": \"string\",\n             \"description\": \"The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA\",\n             \"required\": true\n           }\n         ]\n       }\n     ]\n   }\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n3. Start the runner:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense agent test -c pense/agent/examples/test/config.json -o ./out/run\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOnce you run it, you can open &#96;http://localhost:7860/client/&#96; in your browser. Click **Connect**, and begin talking to the agent through your browser.\n\n\u003e **⚠️ Important**  \n\u003e Avoid using your laptop's speaker and microphone at the same time. Audio from the speaker can be picked up by the microphone, interrupting the agent with its own voice.  \n\u003e **Use a headphone with a built-in microphone.**\n\nThe client UI streams the conversation transcript in real time, while the **Metrics** tab mirrors the live latency statistics reported by the pipeline as shown in the images below:\n\n![Conversation example](images/run_conversation.png)\n\n![Metrics example](images/run_metrics.png)\n\n",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 90
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 27
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".cursorrules/pense.md"
      },
      "score": 0.28064584732055664
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Run Simulation API ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense simulation config from agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config containing system_prompt and llm.model\n        personas: List of persona dicts with description and config (containing gender, language)\n        scenarios: List of scenario dicts with description\n        metrics: List of metric dicts with name and description (for evaluation_criteria)\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from agent config\n    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Build personas list as objects with characteristics, gender, and language\n    persona_list = []\n    for persona in personas:\n        persona_config = persona.get(\"config\") or {}\n        persona_obj = {\n            \"characteristics\": persona.get(\"description\") or persona.get(\"name\"),\n            \"gender\": persona_config.get(\"gender\", \"female\"),\n            \"language\": persona_config.get(\"language\", \"english\"),\n        }\n        if persona_obj[\"characteristics\"]:\n            persona_list.append(persona_obj)\n\n    # Build scenarios list (use description field)\n    scenario_list = [\n        scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n        for scenario in scenarios\n        if scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n    ]",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "text": "    return {\"message\": \"Simulation deleted successfully\"}"

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Run Simulation API ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense simulation config from agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config containing system_prompt and llm.model\n        personas: List of persona dicts with description and config (containing gender, language)\n        scenarios: List of scenario dicts with description\n        metrics: List of metric dicts with name and description (for evaluation_criteria)\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from agent config\n    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Build personas list as objects with characteristics, gender, and language\n    persona_list = []\n    for persona in personas:\n        persona_config = persona.get(\"config\") or {}\n        persona_obj = {\n            \"characteristics\": persona.get(\"description\") or persona.get(\"name\"),\n            \"gender\": persona_config.get(\"gender\", \"female\"),\n            \"language\": persona_config.get(\"language\", \"english\"),\n        }\n        if persona_obj[\"characteristics\"]:\n            persona_list.append(persona_obj)\n\n    # Build scenarios list (use description field)\n    scenario_list = [\n        scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n        for scenario in scenarios\n        if scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n    ]",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "  \"scenarios\": [\n    \"the mother answers all the questions and completes the form, don't ask any further questions after the form is filled\",\n    \"the mother hesitates in directly answering some questions and wants to skip answering them at first but answers later on further probing\",\n    \"the mother is in a rush and ends up answering multiple questions at once that are related to each other when being asked one question; the list of all questions - Name of patient, Address, Telephone\",\n    \"mother ends up occasionally giving partial or invalid answers and corrects them later on in her immediate next message instead of answering the question that she was asked on that turn; first give an incomplete/invalid response and wait for the agent's next message before correcting your previous response\"\n  ] // the different scenarios to evaluate for each persona\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe voice agent is defined in &#96;pense/agent/bot.py&#96;. For now, the default configuration of the bot uses Deepgram for STT, OpenAI for LLM, and Google for TTS. You can configure the bot to use different providers for STT, LLM, and TTS by modifying the &#96;STTConfig&#96;, &#96;LLMConfig&#96;, and &#96;TTSConfig&#96; classes in &#96;pense/agent/bot.py&#96;. We will soon add support for changing the configuration for each provider for each component to override the defaults.\n\nSet the required environment variables for the providers you want to use:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# For the default configuration (Deepgram STT + OpenAI LLM + Google TTS)\nexport DEEPGRAM_API_KEY=your_key\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key\nexport GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/credentials.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense agent simulation -c /path/to/config.json -o /path/to/output\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can use the sample configuration provided in [&#96;pense/agent/examples/sample_short_english.json&#96;](pense/agent/examples/sample_short_english.json) to test the voice agent simulation.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense agent simulation -c examples/sample_short_english.json -o ./out\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSample output:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 824,
            "text": "  ], // different user personas to evaluate for the voice agent"
          },

            "lineNumber": 825,

You can configure the bot to use different providers for STT, LLM, and TTS by modifying the &#96;STTConfig&#96;, &#96;LLMConfig&#96;, and &#96;TTSConfig&#96; classes in &#96;pense/agent/bot.py&#96;. We will soon add support for changing the configuration for each provider for each component to override the defaults."

        "originalContents": "  \"scenarios\": [\n    \"the mother answers all the questions and completes the form, don't ask any further questions after the form is filled\",\n    \"the mother hesitates in directly answering some questions and wants to skip answering them at first but answers later on further probing\",\n    \"the mother is in a rush and ends up answering multiple questions at once that are related to each other when being asked one question; the list of all questions - Name of patient, Address, Telephone\",\n    \"mother ends up occasionally giving partial or invalid answers and corrects them later on in her immediate next message instead of answering the question that she was asked on that turn; first give an incomplete/invalid response and wait for the agent's next message before correcting your previous response\"\n  ] // the different scenarios to evaluate for each persona\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe voice agent is defined in &#96;pense/agent/bot.py&#96;. For now, the default configuration of the bot uses Deepgram for STT, OpenAI for LLM, and Google for TTS. You can configure the bot to use different providers for STT, LLM, and TTS by modifying the &#96;STTConfig&#96;, &#96;LLMConfig&#96;, and &#96;TTSConfig&#96; classes in &#96;pense/agent/bot.py&#96;. We will soon add support for changing the configuration for each provider for each component to override the defaults.\n\nSet the required environment variables for the providers you want to use:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# For the default configuration (Deepgram STT + OpenAI LLM + Google TTS)\nexport DEEPGRAM_API_KEY=your_key\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key\nexport GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/credentials.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense agent simulation -c /path/to/config.json -o /path/to/output\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can use the sample configuration provided in [&#96;pense/agent/examples/sample_short_english.json&#96;](pense/agent/examples/sample_short_english.json) to test the voice agent simulation.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense agent simulation -c examples/sample_short_english.json -o ./out\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSample output:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef create_agent(name: str, config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -\u003e str:\n    \"\"\"Create a new agent and return its UUID.\"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        # Generate UUID for the agent\n        agent_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n        # Serialize config to JSON string for storage\n        config_json = json.dumps(config) if config is not None else None\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO agents (uuid, name, config)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (agent_uuid, name, config_json),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(f\"Created agent with UUID: {agent_uuid}\")\n        return agent_uuid",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "def init_db():"
          },

            "lineNumber": 295
          },

            "lineNumber": 296
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 297,
            "text": "        conn.commit()"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 298,
            "text": "        logger.info(\"Database initialized successfully\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 299
          },

            "lineNumber": 300
          },

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef create_agent(name: str, config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -\u003e str:\n    \"\"\"Create a new agent and return its UUID.\"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        # Generate UUID for the agent\n        agent_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n        # Serialize config to JSON string for storage\n        config_json = json.dumps(config) if config is not None else None\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO agents (uuid, name, config)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (agent_uuid, name, config_json),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(f\"Created agent with UUID: {agent_uuid}\")\n        return agent_uuid",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 327
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 30
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/db.py"

      "score": 0.2413034439086914
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "{\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"instructions for the bot\",\n  \"tools\": [...], // tool schemas available to the agent LLM\n  \"personas\": [\n    // array of persona objects for users in the simulation\n    {\n      \"characteristics\": \"description of user personality, background, and behavior\",\n      \"gender\": \"female\", // gender of the persona (used in user system prompt)\n      \"language\": \"english\" // language for both agent and user in the simulation\n    }\n  ],\n  \"scenarios\": [...], // different simulation scenarios to be run with each user persona\n  \"evaluation_criteria\": [\n    // array of criteria to evaluate each simulation against\n    {\n      \"name\": \"question_completeness\", // unique name for the criterion\n      \"description\": \"Whether all the questions in the form were covered in the conversation\" // description used by LLM judge to evaluate\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"assistant_behavior\",\n      \"description\": \"Whether the assistant asks one concise question per turn; is empathetic and does not repeat the user's answer back to them.\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nEach simulation pairs every persona with every scenario. The runner fans out into separate folders named &#96;simulation_persona_\u003cn\u003e_scenario_\u003cm\u003e&#96; and saves transcripts, evaluation results, plus logs for inspection. Use the sample persona/scenario set from the example config or replace them with your own scripted behaviors (e.g., \"refuses to share phone number until probed twice\"). Use these artifacts to verify that the agent follows your instructions (e.g., responds appropriately, calls the right tools at the right times, and terminates the call correctly), then iterate on the prompts/config as needed.\n\nAfter each simulation, an LLM judge evaluates the transcript against all &#96;evaluation_criteria&#96; defined in the config. Each criterion produces a match (true/false) and reasoning. The results are aggregated across all simulations at the end of the run.\n\nSet the required environment variables:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 616,

            "lineNumber": 630,

            "text": "      \"name\": \"question_completeness\", // unique name for the criterion"

          {

            "lineNumber": 642

          {
            "lineNumber": 643,
            "text": "Each simulation pairs every persona with every scenario. The runner fans out into separate folders named &#96;simulation_persona_\u003cn\u003e_scenario_\u003cm\u003e&#96; and saves transcripts, evaluation results, plus logs for inspection. Use the sample persona/scenario set from the example config or replace them with your own scripted behaviors (e.g., \"refuses to share phone number until probed twice\"). Use these artifacts to verify that the agent follows your instructions (e.g., responds appropriately, calls the right tools at the right times, and terminates the call correctly), then iterate on the prompts/config as needed."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 644
          },

            "text": "After each simulation, an LLM judge evaluates the transcript against all &#96;evaluation_criteria&#96; defined in the config. Each criterion produces a match (true/false) and reasoning. The results are aggregated across all simulations at the end of the run."

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 647,

            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 652
          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 654

          {
            "lineNumber": 655,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "{\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"instructions for the bot\",\n  \"tools\": [...], // tool schemas available to the agent LLM\n  \"personas\": [\n    // array of persona objects for users in the simulation\n    {\n      \"characteristics\": \"description of user personality, background, and behavior\",\n      \"gender\": \"female\", // gender of the persona (used in user system prompt)\n      \"language\": \"english\" // language for both agent and user in the simulation\n    }\n  ],\n  \"scenarios\": [...], // different simulation scenarios to be run with each user persona\n  \"evaluation_criteria\": [\n    // array of criteria to evaluate each simulation against\n    {\n      \"name\": \"question_completeness\", // unique name for the criterion\n      \"description\": \"Whether all the questions in the form were covered in the conversation\" // description used by LLM judge to evaluate\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"assistant_behavior\",\n      \"description\": \"Whether the assistant asks one concise question per turn; is empathetic and does not repeat the user's answer back to them.\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nEach simulation pairs every persona with every scenario. The runner fans out into separate folders named &#96;simulation_persona_\u003cn\u003e_scenario_\u003cm\u003e&#96; and saves transcripts, evaluation results, plus logs for inspection. Use the sample persona/scenario set from the example config or replace them with your own scripted behaviors (e.g., \"refuses to share phone number until probed twice\"). Use these artifacts to verify that the agent follows your instructions (e.g., responds appropriately, calls the right tools at the right times, and terminates the call correctly), then iterate on the prompts/config as needed.\n\nAfter each simulation, an LLM judge evaluates the transcript against all &#96;evaluation_criteria&#96; defined in the config. Each criterion produces a match (true/false) and reasoning. The results are aggregated across all simulations at the end of the run.\n\nSet the required environment variables:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef init_db():\n    \"\"\"Initialize the database and create tables if they don't exist.\"\"\"\n    # Ensure the data directory exists\n    DB_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agents (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                description TEXT,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                agent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                tool_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL,\n                UNIQUE(agent_id, tool_id),\n                FOREIGN KEY (agent_id) REFERENCES agents(uuid),\n                FOREIGN KEY (tool_id) REFERENCES tools(uuid)\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "@contextmanager"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "def get_db_connection():"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    try:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "        yield conn"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    finally:"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "        conn.close()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "def init_db():"

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef init_db():\n    \"\"\"Initialize the database and create tables if they don't exist.\"\"\"\n    # Ensure the data directory exists\n    DB_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agents (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                description TEXT,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_tools (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                agent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                tool_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL,\n                UNIQUE(agent_id, tool_id),\n                FOREIGN KEY (agent_id) REFERENCES agents(uuid),\n                FOREIGN KEY (tool_id) REFERENCES tools(uuid)\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )",
        "range": {

        "contents": "\n\n\nclass AgentResponse(BaseModel):\n    uuid: str\n    name: str\n    config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None\n    created_at: str\n    updated_at: str",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef _parse_agent_row(row: sqlite3.Row) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Parse a database row and deserialize JSON fields.\"\"\"\n    agent = dict(row)\n    # Deserialize config from JSON string\n    if agent.get(\"config\"):\n        agent[\"config\"] = json.loads(agent[\"config\"])\n\n    return agent",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 301,
            "text": "def create_agent(name: str, config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None) -\u003e str:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 316
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 317,
            "text": "        logger.info(f\"Created agent with UUID: {agent_uuid}\")"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 318,
            "text": "        return agent_uuid"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 319
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 320

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef _parse_agent_row(row: sqlite3.Row) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Parse a database row and deserialize JSON fields.\"\"\"\n    agent = dict(row)\n    # Deserialize config from JSON string\n    if agent.get(\"config\"):\n        agent[\"config\"] = json.loads(agent[\"config\"])\n\n    return agent",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 340
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 300
          }

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef update_agent(\n    agent_uuid: str,\n    name: Optional[str] = None,\n    config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n) -\u003e bool:\n    \"\"\"Update an agent. Returns True if the agent was found and updated.\"\"\"\n    # Build dynamic update query\n    updates = []\n    params = []\n\n    if name is not None:\n        updates.append(\"name = ?\")\n        params.append(name)\n    if config is not None:\n        updates.append(\"config = ?\")\n        # Serialize config to JSON string for storage\n        params.append(json.dumps(config))\n\n    if not updates:\n        return False\n\n    updates.append(\"updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\")\n    params.append(agent_uuid)\n\n    query = (\n        f\"UPDATE agents SET {', '.join(updates)} WHERE uuid = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL\"\n    )\n\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        cursor.execute(query, params)\n        conn.commit()\n        updated = cursor.rowcount \u003e 0\n        if updated:\n            logger.info(f\"Updated agent with UUID: {agent_uuid}\")\n        return updated",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 344,
            "text": "def get_all_agents() -\u003e List[Dict[str, Any]]:"
          },

            "lineNumber": 351
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 352,
            "text": "        return [_parse_agent_row(row) for row in rows]"

          },

          },

Returns True if the agent was found and updated.\"\"\"\n    # Build dynamic update query\n    updates = []\n    params = []\n\n    if name is not None:\n        updates.append(\"name = ?\")\n        params.append(name)\n    if config is not None:\n        updates.append(\"config = ?\")\n        # Serialize config to JSON string for storage\n        params.append(json.dumps(config))\n\n    if not updates:\n        return False\n\n    updates.append(\"updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\")\n    params.append(agent_uuid)\n\n    query = (\n        f\"UPDATE agents SET {', '.join(updates)} WHERE uuid = ?     {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel\n\nfrom db import create_agent, get_agent, get_all_agents, update_agent, delete_agent\n\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/agents\", tags=[\"agents\"])\n\n\nclass AgentCreate(BaseModel):\n    name: str\n    config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,

        ],
        "originalContents": "from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel\n\nfrom db import create_agent, get_agent, get_all_agents, update_agent, delete_agent\n\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/agents\", tags=[\"agents\"])\n\n\nclass AgentCreate(BaseModel):\n    name: str\n    config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 43,
            "line": 17
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/agents.py"
      },
      "score": 0.19976085424423218
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "  \"language\": \"english\", // language of the conversation\n  \"tools\": [\n    {\n      \"type\": \"client\",\n      \"name\": \"plan_next_question\",\n      \"description\": \"Optional, only call this tool if the user gave a valid answer to a valid question that you asked; if yes, then which questions have been answered and which question needs to be asked next; if not, don't call this tool at all\",\n      \"disable_interruptions\": false,\n      \"force_pre_tool_speech\": \"auto\",\n      \"assignments\": [],\n      \"tool_call_sound\": null,\n      \"tool_call_sound_behavior\": \"auto\",\n      \"execution_mode\": \"immediate\",\n      \"expects_response\": false,\n      \"response_timeout_secs\": 1,\n      \"parameters\": [\n        {\n          \"id\": \"next_unanswered_question_index\",\n          \"type\": \"integer\",\n          \"value_type\": \"llm_prompt\",\n          \"description\": \"the index of the next unanswered question that should be asked to the user next. don't repeat questions that have been asked before already in the previous responses.\",\n          \"dynamic_variable\": \"\",\n          \"constant_value\": \"\",\n          \"required\": true\n        },\n        {\n          \"id\": \"questions_answered\",\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"description\": \"Optional, the indices of the questions in the full list of questions that have been answered by the user's last response\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"integer\",\n            \"description\": \"the index of each question that got answered by the current response of the user\"\n          },\n          \"required\": true,\n          \"value_type\": \"llm_prompt\"\n        }\n      ]\n    }\n  ], // tools that the voice agent has access to\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 781,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 782,
            "text": "{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 783,
            "text": "  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n\\nYou always speak in english.\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\nOnce all the questions have been answered, end the call by calling the &#96;end_call&#96; tool immediately without asking or saying anything else to the user.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience)\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive true/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked. Only when all the questions have been asked and answered, then, call the &#96;end_call&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\n\", // prompt for the voice agent"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 784,
            "text": "  \"language\": \"english\", // language of the conversation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 785,
            "text": "  \"tools\": ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 786,
            "text": "    {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 787,
            "text": "      \"type\": \"client\","

            "lineNumber": 788,
            "text": "      \"name\": \"plan_next_question\","

            "lineNumber": 789,
            "text": "      \"description\": \"Optional, only call this tool if the user gave a valid answer to a valid question that you asked; if yes, then which questions have been answered and which question needs to be asked next; if not, don't call this tool at all\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 790,

          },

            "lineNumber": 796,
            "text": "      \"expects_response\": false,"
          },

            "lineNumber": 797,

          },

            "text": "      \"parameters\": ["
          },

            "lineNumber": 799,

          },

            "text": "          \"id\": \"next_unanswered_question_index\","

            "text": "          \"description\": \"Optional, the indices of the questions in the full list of questions that have been answered by the user's last response\","
          },

          },

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 818,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 819,
            "text": "      ]"
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 821,
            "text": "  ], // tools that the voice agent has access to"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 822,
            "text": "  \"personas\": ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 823,
            "text": "    \"You are a mother; name is Geeta Prasad, 39 years old lives at Flat 302, Sri Venkateshwara Nilaya, near Hanuman Temple, Indiranagar. Phone number is plus nine one, nine eight triple three, forty-seven twenty-nine zero; never had a stillbirth or a baby who died soon after birth, but has had one baby who died on the third day after delivery; never had three or more miscarriages in a row; in her last pregnancy she wasn’t admitted for high blood pressure or eclampsia; only carrying one baby as per the scan; blood group is O positive, so there are no Rh-related issues; did experience light vaginal spotting once during this pregnancy but otherwise no pelvic mass or complications; uncertain about her exact blood-pressure reading, but recalls that it was around 150/95 mmHg at booking; does not have diabetes, heart disease, kidney disease, or epilepsy; does have asthma and uses an inhaler daily; has never had tuberculosis or any other serious medical condition; longer drinks alcohol or uses substances, having stopped completely after learning she was pregnant; is very shy and reserved and uses short answers to questions\""
          }
        ],
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="search_replace">

<details><summary>Tool use: **search_replace** • Edit file: /Users/aman/Documents/repos/artpark/pense-backend/src/routers/agents.py</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 60, lines removed: 0

```diff
@@ -103,3 +103,63 @@
          raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Agent not found")
      return {"message": "Agent deleted successfully"}
+ 
+ 
+ @router.post("/{agent_uuid}/duplicate", response_model=AgentDuplicateResponse)
+ async def duplicate_agent_endpoint(agent_uuid: str):
+     """
+     Duplicate an agent with all its linked data.
+     
+     This will:
+     - Copy the agent (name with "Copy" suffix, config including speaks_first, data extraction fields, etc.)
+     - Copy all linked tools
+     - Copy all linked tests
+     - Return the new agent UUID
+     """
+     # Get the original agent
+     original_agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)
+     if not original_agent:
+         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Agent not found")
+ 
+     # Create new agent name with "Copy" suffix
+     new_name = f"{original_agent['name']} Copy"
+     
+     # Copy the entire config (includes speaks_first, data extraction fields, llm config, etc.)
+     new_config = original_agent.get("config")
+     if new_config:
+         # Deep copy the config to avoid reference issues
+         import copy
+         new_config = copy.deepcopy(new_config)
+ 
+     # Create the new agent
+     new_agent_uuid = create_agent(
+         name=new_name,
+         config=new_config,
+     )
+ 
+     # Copy all linked tools
+     linked_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent_uuid)
+     for tool in linked_tools:
+         try:
+             add_tool_to_agent(new_agent_uuid, tool["uuid"])
+         except Exception as e:
+             # Log but continue - don't fail the entire duplication
+             import logging
+             logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+             logger.warning(f"Failed to link tool {tool['uuid']} to duplicated agent: {e}")
+ 
+     # Copy all linked tests
+     linked_tests = get_tests_for_agent(agent_uuid)
+     for test in linked_tests:
+         try:
+             add_test_to_agent(new_agent_uuid, test["uuid"])
+         except Exception as e:
+             # Log but continue - don't fail the entire duplication
+             import logging
+             logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+             logger.warning(f"Failed to link test {test['uuid']} to duplicated agent: {e}")
+ 
+     return AgentDuplicateResponse(
+         uuid=new_agent_uuid,
+         message="Agent duplicated successfully with all linked tools and tests",
+     )
  
```

</details>

</tool-use>

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Added the duplicate agent API endpoint. Summary:

## Summary of Changes

### New API Endpoint: `POST /agents/{agent_uuid}/duplicate`

This endpoint duplicates an agent and all its linked data.

### What Gets Duplicated:

1. **Agent itself**:
   - Name: Original name + " Copy" suffix
   - Config: Deep copy of the entire config JSON (includes:
     - `system_prompt`
     - `language`
     - `llm` config (model, provider, etc.)
     - `speaks_first` (if present)
     - Data extraction fields (if present in config)
     - Any other config fields)

2. **Linked Tools**: All tools linked to the original agent are linked to the new agent

3. **Linked Tests**: All tests linked to the original agent are linked to the new agent

### Response:

```json
{
  "uuid": "new-agent-uuid",
  "message": "Agent duplicated successfully with all linked tools and tests"
}
```

### Error Handling:

- Returns 404 if the original agent doesn't exist
- If linking a tool or test fails, logs a warning and continues (doesn't fail the entire duplication)
- Uses deep copy for config to avoid reference issues

### Usage Example:

```bash
POST /agents/{agent_uuid}/duplicate
```

Returns the UUID of the newly created duplicate agent with all its relationships intact.

All changes are complete and linting passes.

---

**Updated naming format:**
- **Before**: `"Agent Name Copy"`
- **After**: `"Agent Name (Copy)"`

The duplicate endpoint now creates agents with the `(Copy)` suffix in parentheses, matching the requested format.

```diff
@@ -114,10 +114,10 @@

- async def duplicate_agent_endpoint(agent_uuid: str):

      """
      Duplicate an agent with all its linked data.
      
      This will:
-     - Copy the agent (name with "Copy" suffix, config including speaks_first, data extraction fields, etc.)
+     - Copy the agent (with the provided name, config including speaks_first, data extraction fields, etc.)
      - Copy all linked tools
      - Copy all linked tests
```

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Updated the duplicate agent API to accept a name in the request body.

## Changes Made

### 1. **Added `AgentDuplicateRequest` Model**
   - Added a request model with a `name` field

### 2. **Updated Endpoint Signature**
   - Changed from `async def duplicate_agent_endpoint(agent_uuid: str)` 
   - To `async def duplicate_agent_endpoint(agent_uuid: str, request: AgentDuplicateRequest)`

### 3. **Updated Logic**
   - Removed automatic name generation with "(Copy)" suffix
   - Now uses the `name` provided in the request body

### API Usage

**Request:**
```bash
POST /agents/{agent_uuid}/duplicate
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "name": "My Duplicated Agent"
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "uuid": "new-agent-uuid",
  "message": "Agent duplicated successfully with all linked tools and tests"
}
```

The duplicate agent will use the name provided in the request body instead of automatically appending "(Copy)".

---

```json
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 911
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 912,
            "text": "Each &#96;simulation_persona_*_scenario_*&#96; directory contains:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 913
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 914,
            "text": "- &#96;audios/&#96;: alternating &#96;*_user.wav&#96; and &#96;*_bot.wav&#96; clips for every turn so you can audit microphone and synthesis quality at each step."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 915,
            "text": "- &#96;logs&#96;: full logs of the simulation including all the pipecat logs"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 916,
            "text": "- &#96;results.log&#96;: only the terminal output of the simulation as shown in the sample output above."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 917,
            "text": "- &#96;metrics.json&#96;: latency traces grouped by processor for both &#96;ttft&#96; (time to first token) and &#96;processing_time&#96;, reported separately for STT, LLM, and TTS providers."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 918,
            "text": "- &#96;stt_outputs.json&#96;: The output of the STT step for each turn in the voice agent being evaluated."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 919,
            "text": "- &#96;tool_calls.json&#96;: chronologically ordered tool calls made by the agent."
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "Creating new audio file at out/agent-sim/simulation_persona_1_scenario_1/audios/1_user.wav\n[User (transcribed)]: Gita Prasad.\n[Agent]: Thank you, Gita. Can you please tell me your full address where you currently live?\nCreating new audio file at out/agent-sim/simulation_persona_1_scenario_1/audios/2_bot.wav\n[User]: Flat 302, Sri Venkateshwara Nilaya, near Hanuman Temple, Indiranagar.\n[User (transcribed)]: 302. Sri Venkateshwara Nilaya, Nirhanuman\n[User (transcribed)]: Temple in Duranagar.\nCreating new audio file at out/agent-sim/simulation_persona_1_scenario_1/audios/2_user.wav\nCreating new audio file at out/agent-sim/simulation_persona_1_scenario_1/audios/3_bot.wav\nAppending audio chunk to out/agent-sim/simulation_persona_1_scenario_1/audios/3_bot.wav\n.\n.\n.\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe output of the evaluation script will be saved in the output directory.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n/path/to/output/\n├── simulation_persona_1_scenario_1\n│   ├── audios\n│   │    ├── 0_user.wav\n│   │    ├── 1_bot.wav\n│   │    ├── 1_user.wav\n│   │    ...\n│   ├── logs\n│   ├── results.log\n│   ├── metrics.json\n│   ├── stt_outputs.json\n│   ├── tool_calls.json\n│   ├── transcripts.json\n├── simulation_persona_1_scenario_2\n├── simulation_persona_1_scenario_3\n└── ...\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nEach &#96;simulation_persona_*_scenario_*&#96; directory contains:\n\n- &#96;audios/&#96;: alternating &#96;*_user.wav&#96; and &#96;*_bot.wav&#96; clips for every turn so you can audit microphone and synthesis quality at each step.\n- &#96;logs&#96;: full logs of the simulation including all the pipecat logs\n- &#96;results.log&#96;: only the terminal output of the simulation as shown in the sample output above.\n- &#96;metrics.json&#96;: latency traces grouped by processor for both &#96;ttft&#96; (time to first token) and &#96;processing_time&#96;, reported separately for STT, LLM, and TTS providers.\n- &#96;stt_outputs.json&#96;: The output of the STT step for each turn in the voice agent being evaluated.\n",

        "contents": "\ndef _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e\n\n    ...\n\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"\n    results_file = output_dir / \"results.csv\"\n\n    if metrics_file.exists():\n        with open(metrics_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n            metrics_data = json.load(f)\n\n    # Parse results.csv for aggregated scores\n    if results_file.exists():\n        import csv\n\n        with open(results_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n            reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n            results_data = list(reader)\n\n    # Find simulation directories and parse their results\n    for item in output_dir.iterdir():\n        if item.is_dir() and item.name.startswith(\"simulation_persona_\"):\n            sim_name = item.name\n            eval_results_file = item / \"evaluation_results.csv\"\n            transcript_file = item / \"transcript.json\"\n\n            eval_results = []\n            if eval_results_file.exists():\n                import csv\n\n                with open(eval_results_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                    reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n                    for row in reader:\n                        eval_results.append(\n                            {\n                                \"name\": row.get(\"name\"),\n                                \"match\": row.get(\"match\", \"\").lower() == \"true\",\n                                \"reasoning\": row.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                            }\n                        )\n\n            # Parse transcript.json if it exists\n            transcript = None\n            if transcript_file.exists():\n                try:\n                    with open(transcript_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                        transcript = json.load(f)\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Failed to ",

            "text": "                    logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse transcript.json for {sim_name}: {e}\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 679
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 680,
            "text": "            # Extract persona and scenario indices from directory name"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"\n    results_file = output_dir / \"results.csv\"\n\n    if metrics_file.exists():\n        with open(metrics_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n            metrics_data = json.load(f)\n\n    # Parse results.csv for aggregated scores\n    if results_file.exists():\n        import csv\n\n        with open(results_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n            reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n            results_data = list(reader)\n\n    # Find simulation directories and parse their results\n    for item in output_dir.iterdir():\n        if item.is_dir() and item.name.startswith(\"simulation_persona_\"):\n            sim_name = item.name\n            eval_results_file = item / \"evaluation_results.csv\"\n            transcript_file = item / \"transcript.json\"\n\n            eval_results = []\n            if eval_results_file.exists():\n                import csv\n\n                with open(eval_results_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                    reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n                    for row in reader:\n                        eval_results.append(\n                            {\n                                \"name\": row.get(\"name\"),\n                                \"match\": row.get(\"match\", \"\").lower() == \"true\",\n                                \"reasoning\": row.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                            }\n                        )\n\n            # Parse transcript.json if it exists\n            transcript = None\n            if transcript_file.exists():\n                try:\n                    with open(transcript_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                        transcript = json.load(f)\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Failed to ",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {

            "column": 5,
```
