```json
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "   \"evaluation_criteria\": evaluation_criteria,\n    }\n\n\ndef _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "Finished streaming audio: /path/to/audio_1.wav\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript\u003e.\n--------------------------------\nCreated new user transcript\nStarting new audio streaming: /path/to/audio_2.wav\nFinished streaming audio: /path/to/audio_2.wav\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript_part_1\u003e,\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript_part_2\u003e,\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe output of the evaluation script will be saved in the output directory.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n/path/to/output\n├── sarvam_english\n│   ├── logs\n│   ├── results.log\n│   ├── results.csv\n│   └── metrics.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;results.csv&#96; will have the following columns:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csv\nid,gt,pred,wer,string_similarity,llm_judge_score,llm_judge_reasoning\n3_1_english_baseline,\"Please write Rekha Kumari, sister.\", Please write Reha Kumari's sister.,0.4,0.927536231884058,False,\"The source says 'Rekha Kumari, sister' which indicates the name is 'Rekha Kumari' and she is a sister. The transcription says 'Reha Kumari's sister', which changes the name to 'Reha Kumari' and refers to her sister, not Rekha Kumari herself. The name is different ('Rekha' vs 'Reha') and the relationship is also changed (from identifying Rekha Kumari as the sister to referring to the sister of Reha Kumari). Therefore, the values do not match.\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe definition of all the metrics we compute are stored in [&#96;pense/stt/metrics.py&#96;](pense/stt/metrics.py).\n\n&#96;metrics.json&#96; will have the following format:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n",

        "originalContents": "Finished streaming audio: /path/to/audio_1.wav\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript\u003e.\n--------------------------------\nCreated new user transcript\nStarting new audio streaming: /path/to/audio_2.wav\nFinished streaming audio: /path/to/audio_2.wav\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript_part_1\u003e,\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript_part_2\u003e,\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe output of the evaluation script will be saved in the output directory.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n/path/to/output\n├── sarvam_english\n│   ├── logs\n│   ├── results.log\n│   ├── results.csv\n│   └── metrics.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;results.csv&#96; will have the following columns:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csv\nid,gt,pred,wer,string_similarity,llm_judge_score,llm_judge_reasoning\n3_1_english_baseline,\"Please write Rekha Kumari, sister.\", Please write Reha Kumari's sister.,0.4,0.927536231884058,False,\"The source says 'Rekha Kumari, sister' which indicates the name is 'Rekha Kumari' and she is a sister. The transcription says 'Reha Kumari's sister', which changes the name to 'Reha Kumari' and refers to her sister, not Rekha Kumari herself. The name is different ('Rekha' vs 'Reha') and the relationship is also changed (from identifying Rekha Kumari as the sister to referring to the sister of Reha Kumari). Therefore, the values do not match.\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe definition of all the metrics we compute are stored in [&#96;pense/stt/metrics.py&#96;](pense/stt/metrics.py).\n\n&#96;metrics.json&#96; will have the following format:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n",

    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "Every user/bot audio turn is persisted inside &#96;\u003coutput_dir\u003e/audios&#96; (see a sample output folder for the exact layout). The terminal also logs each transcript message and every function/tool call issued by the LLM so you can audit the interaction without leaving your shell.\n\n&#96;transcript.json&#96; in the output directory has the full transcript with function calls.\n\nYou can checkout &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/sample_output&#96; as a sample output of the agent conversation which contains all the audio turns and logs from the conversation.\n\n## Speech To Text (STT)\n\nTo evaluate different STT providers, first make sure to organize the input data in the following structure:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n├── /path/to/data\n│   └── stt.csv\n│   └── audios/wav\n│       └── audio_1.wav\n│       └── audio_2.wav\n│   └── audios/pcm16\n│       └── audio_1.wav\n│       └── audio_2.wav\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;stt.csv&#96; should have the following format:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csv\nid,text\naudio_1,\"Hi\"\naudio_2,\"Madam, my name is Geeta Shankar\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nStore the audios in both &#96;wav&#96; and &#96;pcm16&#96; formats as different providers support different formats. The evaluation script will automatically select the right format for the provider you choose to evaluate.\n\nSet the required environment variables for the STT provider you want to evaluate:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# For Sarvam\nexport SARVAM_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For Deepgram\nexport DEEPGRAM_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For OpenAI\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For Google\nexport GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/credentials.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense stt eval -p \u003cprovider\u003e -l \u003clanguage\u003e -i /path/to/data -o /path/to/output -d\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can use the sample inputs provided in [&#96;pense/stt/examples/sample_input&#96;](pense/stt/examples/sample_input) to test the evaluation script.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense stt eval -p sarvam -l english -i samples -o ./out -d\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSample output:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nCreated new user transcript\nStarting new audio streaming: /path/to/audio_1.wav\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "![Metrics example](images/run_metrics.png)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "Every user/bot audio turn is persisted inside &#96;\u003coutput_dir\u003e/audios&#96; (see a sample output folder for the exact layout). The terminal also logs each transcript message and every function/tool call issued by the LLM so you can audit the interaction without leaving your shell."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "&#96;transcript.json&#96; in the output directory has the full transcript with function calls."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "You can checkout &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/sample_output&#96; as a sample output of the agent conversation which contains all the audio turns and logs from the conversation."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "## Speech To Text (STT)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "To evaluate different STT providers, first make sure to organize the input data in the following structure:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "├── /path/to/data"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "│   └── stt.csv"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "│   └── audios/wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "│       └── audio_1.wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "│       └── audio_2.wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "│   └── audios/pcm16"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "│       └── audio_1.wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "│       └── audio_2.wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "&#96;stt.csv&#96; should have the following format:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;csv"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "id,text"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "audio_1,\"Hi\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "audio_2,\"Madam, my name is Geeta Shankar\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "Store the audios in both &#96;wav&#96; and &#96;pcm16&#96; formats as different providers support different formats. The evaluation script will automatically select the right format for the provider you choose to evaluate."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "Set the required environment variables for the STT provider you want to evaluate:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "# For Sarvam"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "export SARVAM_API_KEY=your_key"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": "# For Deepgram"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": "export DEEPGRAM_API_KEY=your_key"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 130,
            "text": "# For OpenAI"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131,
            "text": "export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 133,
            "text": "# For Google"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 134,
            "text": "export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/credentials.json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 135,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138,
            "text": "pense stt eval -p \u003cprovider\u003e -l \u003clanguage\u003e -i /path/to/data -o /path/to/output -d"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 139,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 141,
            "text": "You can use the sample inputs provided in [&#96;pense/stt/examples/sample_input&#96;](pense/stt/examples/sample_input) to test the evaluation script."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 142
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 143,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 144,
            "text": "pense stt eval -p sarvam -l english -i samples -o ./out -d"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,
            "text": "Sample output:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "Created new user transcript"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "Starting new audio streaming: /path/to/audio_1.wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152,
            "text": "Finished streaming audio: /path/to/audio_1.wav"
          }

        "originalContents": "Every user/bot audio turn is persisted inside &#96;\u003coutput_dir\u003e/audios&#96; (see a sample output folder for the exact layout). The terminal also logs each transcript message and every function/tool call issued by the LLM so you can audit the interaction without leaving your shell.\n\n&#96;transcript.json&#96; in the output directory has the full transcript with function calls.\n\nYou can checkout &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/sample_output&#96; as a sample output of the agent conversation which contains all the audio turns and logs from the conversation.\n\n## Speech To Text (STT)\n\nTo evaluate different STT providers, first make sure to organize the input data in the following structure:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n├── /path/to/data\n│   └── stt.csv\n│   └── audios/wav\n│       └── audio_1.wav\n│       └── audio_2.wav\n│   └── audios/pcm16\n│       └── audio_1.wav\n│       └── audio_2.wav\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;stt.csv&#96; should have the following format:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csv\nid,text\naudio_1,\"Hi\"\naudio_2,\"Madam, my name is Geeta Shankar\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nStore the audios in both &#96;wav&#96; and &#96;pcm16&#96; formats as different providers support different formats. The evaluation script will automatically select the right format for the provider you choose to evaluate.\n\nSet the required environment variables for the STT provider you want to evaluate:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# For Sarvam\nexport SARVAM_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For Deepgram\nexport DEEPGRAM_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For OpenAI\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For Google\nexport GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/credentials.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense stt eval -p \u003cprovider\u003e -l \u003clanguage\u003e -i /path/to/data -o /path/to/output -d\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can use the sample inputs provided in [&#96;pense/stt/examples/sample_input&#96;](pense/stt/examples/sample_input) to test the evaluation script.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense stt eval -p sarvam -l english -i samples -o ./out -d\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSample output:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nCreated new user transcript\nStarting new audio streaming: /path/to/audio_1.wav\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def _run_pense_test(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM test\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n\n\n    ...\n\n\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(output_dir):\n        for file in files:\n            file_path = Path(root) / file\n            if file == \"results.json\" and results_data is None:\n                with open(file_path, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                    results_data = json.load(f)\n            elif file == \"metrics.json\" and metrics_data is None:\n                with open(file_path, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                    metrics_data = json.load(f)\n\n    # Upload results to S3\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(output_dir):\n        for file in files:\n            local_file_path = Path(root) / file\n            relative_path = local_file_path.relative_to(output_dir)\n            s3_key = f\"{s3_prefix}/{relative_path}\"\n            s3.upload_file(str(local_file_path), s3_bucket, s3_key)\n\n    # Parse metrics\n    total_tests = 0\n    passed = 0\n    failed = 0\n\n    if metrics_data and isinstance(metrics_data, dict):\n        total_tests = metrics_data.get(\"total\", 0)\n        passed = metrics_data.get(\"passed\", 0)\n        failed = total_tests - passed\n\n    # Parse results\n    test_results = []",
        "detailedLines": [

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThen launch simulations:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense llm simulations run \\\n  -c /path/to/config.json \\\n  -o /path/to/output \\\n  -m \u003cmodel_name\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can use the sample configuration provided in [&#96;pense/llm/examples/simulation/config.json&#96;](pense/llm/examples/simulation/config.json) to test the simulation.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense llm simulations run \\\n  -c examples/simulation/config.json \\\n  -o ./out \\\n  -m gpt-4.1\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSample output:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n--------------------------------\nRunning simulation simulation_persona_1_scenario_1\nPersona:\nYou are a mother; name is Geeta Prasad, 39 years old lives at Flat 302, Sri Venkateshwara Nilaya, near Hanuman Temple, Indiranagar. Phone number is plus nine one, nine eight triple three, forty-seven twenty-nine zero; never had a stillbirth or a baby who died soon after birth, but has had one baby who died on the third day after delivery; never had three or more miscarriages in a row; in her last pregnancy she wasn't admitted for high blood pressure or eclampsia; only carrying one baby as per the scan; blood group is O positive, so there are no Rh-related issues; did experience light vaginal spotting once during this pregnancy but otherwise no pelvic mass or complications; uncertain about her exact blood-pressure reading, but recalls that it was around 150/95 mmHg at booking; does not have diabetes, heart disease, kidney disease, or epilepsy; does have asthma and uses an inhaler daily; has never had tuberculosis or any other serious medical condition; longer drinks alcohol or uses substances, having stopped completely after learning she was pregnant; is very shy and reserved and uses short answers to questions\nGender: female\nLanguage: english\nScenario:\nthe mother answers all the questions and completes the form, don't ask any further questions after the form is filled\n--------------------------------\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 647,
            "text": "Set the required environment variables:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 648
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 649,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 650,
            "text": "export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 651,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 652
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 653,
            "text": "Then launch simulations:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 654
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 655,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 656,
            "text": "pense llm simulations run \\"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 657,
            "text": "  -c /path/to/config.json \\"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 658,
            "text": "  -o /path/to/output \\"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 659,
            "text": "  -m \u003cmodel_name\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 660,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 661
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 662,
            "text": "You can use the sample configuration provided in [&#96;pense/llm/examples/simulation/config.json&#96;](pense/llm/examples/simulation/config.json) to test the simulation."

          },

            "lineNumber": 664,

            "text": "  -m gpt-4.1"

          },

            "lineNumber": 677,
            "text": "You are a mother; name is Geeta Prasad, 39 years old lives at Flat 302, Sri Venkateshwara Nilaya, near Hanuman Temple, Indiranagar. Phone number is plus nine one, nine eight triple three, forty-seven twenty-nine zero; never had a stillbirth or a baby who died soon after birth, but has had one baby who died on the third day after delivery; never had three or more miscarriages in a row; in her last pregnancy she wasn't admitted for high blood pressure or eclampsia; only carrying one baby as per the scan; blood group is O positive, so there are no Rh-related issues; did experience light vaginal spotting once during this pregnancy but otherwise no pelvic mass or complications; uncertain about her exact blood-pressure reading, but recalls that it was around 150/95 mmHg at booking; does not have diabetes, heart disease, kidney disease, or epilepsy; does have asthma and uses an inhaler daily; has never had tuberculosis or any other serious medical condition; longer drinks alcohol or uses substances, having stopped completely after learning she was pregnant; is very shy and reserved and uses short answers to questions"
          },

            "lineNumber": 678,
            "text": "Gender: female"
          },
          {

          },

            "lineNumber": 680,
            "text": "Scenario:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 681,
            "text": "the mother answers all the questions and completes the form, don't ask any further questions after the form is filled"
          },

            "lineNumber": 682,
            "text": "--------------------------------"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 683,
            "text": "[Agent]: To get started with your antenatal visit details, could you please tell me your full name, including both your first name and last name?"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThen launch simulations:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense llm simulations run \\\n  -c /path/to/config.json \\\n  -o /path/to/output \\\n  -m \u003cmodel_name\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can use the sample configuration provided in [&#96;pense/llm/examples/simulation/config.json&#96;](pense/llm/examples/simulation/config.json) to test the simulation.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense llm simulations run \\\n  -c examples/simulation/config.json \\\n  -o ./out \\\n  -m gpt-4.1\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSample output:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n--------------------------------\nRunning simulation simulation_persona_1_scenario_1\nPersona:\nYou are a mother; name is Geeta Prasad, 39 years old lives at Flat 302, Sri Venkateshwara Nilaya, near Hanuman Temple, Indiranagar. Phone number is plus nine one, nine eight triple three, forty-seven twenty-nine zero; never had a stillbirth or a baby who died soon after birth, but has had one baby who died on the third day after delivery; never had three or more miscarriages in a row; in her last pregnancy she wasn't admitted for high blood pressure or eclampsia; only carrying one baby as per the scan; blood group is O positive, so there are no Rh-related issues; did experience light vaginal spotting once during this pregnancy but otherwise no pelvic mass or complications; uncertain about her exact blood-pressure reading, but recalls that it was around 150/95 mmHg at booking; does not have diabetes, heart disease, kidney disease, or epilepsy; does have asthma and uses an inhaler daily; has never had tuberculosis or any other serious medical condition; longer drinks alcohol or uses substances, having stopped completely after learning she was pregnant; is very shy and reserved and uses short answers to questions\nGender: female\nLanguage: english\nScenario:\nthe mother answers all the questions and completes the form, don't ask any further questions after the form is filled\n--------------------------------\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef evaluate_provider(\n    run_id: str,\n    provider: str,\n    language: str,\n    input_dir: Path,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    port: int,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n) -\u003e ProviderResult:\n    \"\"\"Evaluate a single STT provider.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        # Run pense STT eval command\n        eval_cmd = [\n            \"pense\",\n            \"stt\",\n            \"eval\",\n            \"-p\",\n            provider,\n            \"-l\",\n            language,\n            \"-i\",\n            str(input_dir),\n            \"-o\",\n            str(output_dir),\n            \"--port\",\n            str(port),\n        ]\n\n        logger.info(f\"Running {run_id} with command: {' '.join(eval_cmd)}\")\n\n        subprocess.run(\n            eval_cmd,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            check=True,\n            cwd=str(output_dir.parent),\n        )\n\n        # Find the provider-specific output directory\n        provider_output_dir = None\n        for item in output_dir.iterdir():\n            if item.is_dir() and provider in item.name.lower():\n                provider_output_dir = item\n                break\n\n        # Upload STT eval results to S3\n        results_prefix = f\"stt/evals/{run_id}/outputs/{provider}\"\n\n        # Read metrics.json and results.csv before uploading\n        metrics_data = None\n        results_data = None\n\n        metrics_file = provider_output_dir / \"metrics.json\"\n        results_file = provider_output_dir / \"results.csv\"\n\n        if metrics_file.exists():\n            with open(metrics_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                metrics_data = json.load(f)\n\n        if results_file.exists():\n            results_data = []\n            with open(results_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n                for row in reader:\n                    results_data.append(dict(row))",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef evaluate_provider(\n    run_id: str,\n    provider: str,\n    language: str,\n    input_dir: Path,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    port: int,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n) -\u003e ProviderResult:\n    \"\"\"Evaluate a single STT provider.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        # Run pense STT eval command\n        eval_cmd = [\n            \"pense\",\n            \"stt\",\n            \"eval\",\n            \"-p\",\n            provider,\n            \"-l\",\n            language,\n            \"-i\",\n            str(input_dir),\n            \"-o\",\n            str(output_dir),\n            \"--port\",\n            str(port),\n        ]\n\n        logger.info(f\"Running {run_id} with command: {' '.join(eval_cmd)}\")\n\n        subprocess.run(\n            eval_cmd,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            check=True,\n            cwd=str(output_dir.parent),\n        )\n\n        # Find the provider-specific output directory\n        provider_output_dir = None\n        for item in output_dir.iterdir():\n            if item.is_dir() and provider in item.name.lower():\n                provider_output_dir = item\n                break\n\n        # Upload STT eval results to S3\n        results_prefix = f\"stt/evals/{run_id}/outputs/{provider}\"\n\n        # Read metrics.json and results.csv before uploading\n        metrics_data = None\n        results_data = None\n\n        metrics_file = provider_output_dir / \"metrics.json\"\n        results_file = provider_output_dir / \"results.csv\"\n\n        if metrics_file.exists():\n            with open(metrics_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                metrics_data = json.load(f)\n\n        if results_file.exists():\n            results_data = []\n            with open(results_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n                for row in reader:\n                    results_data.append(dict(row))",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {

          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 32
          }

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "&#96;logs&#96; contains the full logs of the simulation including all the pipecat logs whereas &#96;results.log&#96; contains only the terminal output of the simulation as shown in the sample output above.\n\nYou can checkout [&#96;pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output&#96;](pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output) to see a sample output of the simulation.\n\n## Voice agent simulations\n\nTo run agent simulations with all the three components - STT, LLM, and TTS - prepare a JSON configuration file as shown below.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n\\nYou always speak in english.\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\nOnce all the questions have been answered, end the call by calling the &#96;end_call&#96; tool immediately without asking or saying anything else to the user.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience)\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive true/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked. Only when all the questions have been asked and answered, then, call the &#96;end_call&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\n\", // prompt for the voice agent\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 767,
            "text": "    \"std\": 0.0,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 768,
            "text": "    \"values\": [1.0, 1.0, 1.0]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 769,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 770,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 771,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 772
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 773,
            "text": "&#96;logs&#96; contains the full logs of the simulation including all the pipecat logs whereas &#96;results.log&#96; contains only the terminal output of the simulation as shown in the sample output above."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 774
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 775,
            "text": "You can checkout [&#96;pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output&#96;](pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output) to see a sample output of the simulation."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 776
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 777,
            "text": "## Voice agent simulations"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 778
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 779,
            "text": "To run agent simulations with all the three components - STT, LLM, and TTS - prepare a JSON configuration file as shown below."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 780
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 781,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 782,
            "text": "{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 783,
            "text": "  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n\\nYou always speak in english.\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\nOnce all the questions have been answered, end the call by calling the &#96;end_call&#96; tool immediately without asking or saying anything else to the user.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience)\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive true/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked. Only when all the questions have been asked and answered, then, call the &#96;end_call&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\n\", // prompt for the voice agent"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 784,
            "text": "  \"language\": \"english\", // language of the conversation"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "&#96;logs&#96; contains the full logs of the simulation including all the pipecat logs whereas &#96;results.log&#96; contains only the terminal output of the simulation as shown in the sample output above.\n\nYou can checkout [&#96;pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output&#96;](pense/llm/examples/simulation/sample_output) to see a sample output of the simulation.\n\n## Voice agent simulations\n\nTo run agent simulations with all the three components - STT, LLM, and TTS - prepare a JSON configuration file as shown below.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n\\nYou always speak in english.\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\nOnce all the questions have been answered, end the call by calling the &#96;end_call&#96; tool immediately without asking or saying anything else to the user.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience)\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive true/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked. Only when all the questions have been asked and answered, then, call the &#96;end_call&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\n\", // prompt for the voice agent\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef evaluate_tts_provider(\n    run_id: str,\n    provider: str,\n    language: str,\n    input_csv: Path,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    port: int,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n) -\u003e ProviderResult:\n    \"\"\"Evaluate a single TTS provider.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        # Run pense TTS eval command\n        eval_cmd = [\n            \"pense\",\n            \"tts\",\n            \"eval\",\n            \"-p\",\n            provider,\n            \"-l\",\n            language,\n            \"-i\",\n            str(input_csv),\n            \"-o\",\n            str(output_dir),\n            \"--port\",\n            str(port),\n        ]\n\n        logger.info(f\"Running TTS eval {run_id} with command: {' '.join(eval_cmd)}\")\n\n        subprocess.run(\n            eval_cmd,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            check=True,\n            cwd=str(output_dir.parent),\n        )\n\n        # Find the provider-specific output directory\n        provider_output_dir = None\n        for item in output_dir.iterdir():\n            if item.is_dir() and provider in item.name.lower():\n                provider_output_dir = item\n                break\n\n        if provider_output_dir is None:\n            # Try to find any directory in output_dir\n            dirs = [d for d in output_dir.iterdir() if d.is_dir()]\n            if dirs:\n                provider_output_dir = dirs[0]\n            else:\n                raise Exception(\n                    f\"Could not find provider output directory for {provider}\"\n                )\n\n        # Upload TTS eval results to S3\n        results_prefix = f\"tts/evals/{run_id}/outputs/{provider}\"\n\n        # Read metrics.json and results.csv before uploading\n        metrics_data = None\n        results_data = None\n\n        metrics_file = provider_output_dir / \"metrics.json\"\n        results_file = provider_output_dir / \"results.csv\"\n\n        if metrics_file.exists():\n            with open(metrics_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                metrics_data = json.load(f)",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef evaluate_tts_provider(\n    run_id: str,\n    provider: str,\n    language: str,\n    input_csv: Path,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    port: int,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n) -\u003e ProviderResult:\n    \"\"\"Evaluate a single TTS provider.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        # Run pense TTS eval command\n        eval_cmd = [\n            \"pense\",\n            \"tts\",\n            \"eval\",\n            \"-p\",\n            provider,\n            \"-l\",\n            language,\n            \"-i\",\n            str(input_csv),\n            \"-o\",\n            str(output_dir),\n            \"--port\",\n            str(port),\n        ]\n\n        logger.info(f\"Running TTS eval {run_id} with command: {' '.join(eval_cmd)}\")\n\n        subprocess.run(\n            eval_cmd,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            check=True,\n            cwd=str(output_dir.parent),\n        )\n\n        # Find the provider-specific output directory\n        provider_output_dir = None\n        for item in output_dir.iterdir():\n            if item.is_dir() and provider in item.name.lower():\n                provider_output_dir = item\n                break\n\n        if provider_output_dir is None:\n            # Try to find any directory in output_dir\n            dirs = [d for d in output_dir.iterdir() if d.is_dir()]\n            if dirs:\n                provider_output_dir = dirs[0]\n            else:\n                raise Exception(\n                    f\"Could not find provider output directory for {provider}\"\n                )\n\n        # Upload TTS eval results to S3\n        results_prefix = f\"tts/evals/{run_id}/outputs/{provider}\"\n\n        # Read metrics.json and results.csv before uploading\n        metrics_data = None\n        results_data = None\n\n        metrics_file = provider_output_dir / \"metrics.json\"\n        results_file = provider_output_dir / \"results.csv\"\n\n        if metrics_file.exists():\n            with open(metrics_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                metrics_data = json.load(f)",
```

```diff
+                     if part == "persona" and i + 1 &lt; len(parts):
+                         persona_idx = parts[i + 1]
+                     if part == "scenario" and i + 1 &lt; len(parts):
+                         scenario_idx = parts[i + 1]
+ 
+                 simulation_results.append(
+                     {
+                         "simulation_name": sim_name,
+                         "persona": f"persona_{persona_idx}",
+                         "scenario": f"scenario_{scenario_idx}",
+                         "evaluation_results": eval_results,
+                         "transcript": transcript,
+                     }
+                 )
+ 
              # Extract persona and scenario indices from directory name
              # Format: simulation_persona_X_scenario_Y
```

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


## Summary of Changes

### 1. **Updated `SimulationCaseResult` Model**
   - Added `transcript: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None` to include conversation history

### 2. **Updated Simulation Results Parsing**
   - Switched to recursive search using `os.walk()` to find simulation directories (handles nested structures)
   - Added parsing of `transcript.json` from each simulation directory
   - Includes error handling if transcript parsing fails

### 3. **Transcript Content**
   The transcript includes:
   - Conversation messages with `role` (assistant/user/tool) and `content`
   - Tool calls with function names and arguments
   - Full conversation history for each simulation

### API Response Example

Now the simulation results will include the transcript:

```json
{
  "simulation_results": [
    {
      "simulation_name": "simulation_persona_1_scenario_1",
      "persona": "persona_1",
      "scenario": "scenario_1",
      "evaluation_results": [...],
      "transcript": [
        {
          "role": "assistant",
          "content": "..."
        },
        {
          "role": "user",
          "content": "..."
        },
        {
          "role": "assistant",
          "tool_calls": [
            {
              "id": "tool_plan_next_question_...",
              "function": {
                "name": "plan_next_question",
                "arguments": "{...}"
              },
              "type": "function"
            }
          ]
        },
        ...
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

The transcript includes the full conversation history with all tool calls for each simulation, matching the structure shown in your example file.

```json
{
  "codeResults": [
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Run Simulation API ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense simulation config from agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config containing system_prompt and llm.model\n        personas: List of persona dicts with description and config (containing gender, language)\n        scenarios: List of scenario dicts with description\n        metrics: List of metric dicts with name and description (for evaluation_criteria)\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from agent config\n    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Build personas list as objects with characteristics, gender, and language\n    persona_list = []\n    for persona in personas:\n        persona_config = persona.get(\"config\") or {}\n        persona_obj = {\n            \"characteristics\": persona.get(\"description\") or persona.get(\"name\"),\n            \"gender\": persona_config.get(\"gender\", \"female\"),\n            \"language\": persona_config.get(\"language\", \"english\"),\n        }\n        if persona_obj[\"characteristics\"]:\n            persona_list.append(persona_obj)\n\n    # Build scenarios list (use description field)\n    scenario_list = [\n        scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n        for scenario in scenarios\n        if scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n    ]",
        "detailedLines": [

            "lineNumber": 494,
            "text": "    \"\"\""

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 497,
            "text": "    Args:"

            "lineNumber": 498,

            "lineNumber": 499,

          {
            "lineNumber": 501,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 502,
            "text": "    \"\"\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 503,
            "text": "    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}"

            "lineNumber": 504
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 505,
            "text": "    # Get model from agent config"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 506,
            "text": "    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 507,
            "text": "    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 508
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 509,
            "text": "    # Get tools from agent_tools table"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 510,
            "text": "    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 511,

            "lineNumber": 512,

          },

          },
          {

            "text": "            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 516,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 517,
            "text": "        for tool in agent_tools"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 518,
            "text": "    ]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 519
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 520,

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 528,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 529,

            "lineNumber": 531
          },

            "lineNumber": 532,

          },

          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Run Simulation API ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense simulation config from agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config containing system_prompt and llm.model\n        personas: List of persona dicts with description and config (containing gender, language)\n        scenarios: List of scenario dicts with description\n        metrics: List of metric dicts with name and description (for evaluation_criteria)\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from agent config\n    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Build personas list as objects with characteristics, gender, and language\n    persona_list = []\n    for persona in personas:\n        persona_config = persona.get(\"config\") or {}\n        persona_obj = {\n            \"characteristics\": persona.get(\"description\") or persona.get(\"name\"),\n            \"gender\": persona_config.get(\"gender\", \"female\"),\n            \"language\": persona_config.get(\"language\", \"english\"),\n        }\n        if persona_obj[\"characteristics\"]:\n            persona_list.append(persona_obj)\n\n    # Build scenarios list (use description field)\n    scenario_list = [\n        scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n        for scenario in scenarios\n        if scenario.get(\"description\") or scenario.get(\"name\")\n    ]",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 555
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 475
          }

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"",

Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    #     {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n\n\n        ...\n\n\n        try:\n            # Build pense config\n            pense_config = _build_pense_simulation_config(\n                agent, personas, scenarios, metrics\n            )\n            model_to_use = pense_config[\"params\"][\"model\"]\n\n            # Create output directory\n            output_dir = temp_path / \"output\"\n            output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n            # Run pense simulation\n            results_prefix = f\"simulations/runs/{task_id}\"\n            result = _run_pense_simulation(\n                model=model_to_use,\n                pense_config=pense_config,\n                output_dir=output_dir,\n                s3_bucket=s3_bucket,\n                s3_prefix=results_prefix,\n                log_prefix=f\"Simulation {task_id}\",\n            )\n\n            # Update job with results\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\n                    \"total_simulations\": result[\"total_simulations\"],\n                    \"metrics\": result[\"metrics\"],\n                    \"simulation_results\": result[\"simulation_results\"],\n                    \"results_s3_prefix\": results_prefix,\n                    \"error\": result[\"error\"],\n                },\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Simulation task {task_id} completed: \"\n                f\"{result['total_simulations']} simulation(s) run\"\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\"error\": f\"Unexpected error during simulation: {str(e)}\"},\n            )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        update_simulation_job(\n            task_id,\n            status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n            results={\"error\": f\"Task failed: {str(e)}\"},\n        )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 727,
            "text": "def run_simulation_task("

            "isSignature": true,

            "text": "    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],"

            "text": "    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 732,
            "text": "    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 733,
            "text": "    s3_bucket: str,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,

            "lineNumber": 735,
            "text": "    "
          },

            "lineNumber": 746
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 747,
            "text": "        temp_path = output_base_dir / task_id"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 748,
            "text": "        temp_path.mkdir(exist_ok=True)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 749
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 750,
            "text": "        try:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 751,
            "text": "            # Build pense config"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 752,
            "text": "            pense_config = _build_pense_simulation_config("

        "originalContents": "\n\n        try:\n            # Build pense config\n            pense_config = _build_pense_simulation_config(\n                agent, personas, scenarios, metrics\n            )\n            model_to_use = pense_config[\"params\"][\"model\"]\n\n            # Create output directory\n            output_dir = temp_path / \"output\"\n            output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n            # Run pense simulation\n            results_prefix = f\"simulations/runs/{task_id}\"\n            result = _run_pense_simulation(\n                model=model_to_use,\n                pense_config=pense_config,\n                output_dir=output_dir,\n                s3_bucket=s3_bucket,\n                s3_prefix=results_prefix,\n                log_prefix=f\"Simulation {task_id}\",\n            )\n\n            # Update job with results\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\n                    \"total_simulations\": result[\"total_simulations\"],\n                    \"metrics\": result[\"metrics\"],\n                    \"simulation_results\": result[\"simulation_results\"],\n                    \"results_s3_prefix\": results_prefix,\n                    \"error\": result[\"error\"],\n                },\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Simulation task {task_id} completed: \"\n                f\"{result['total_simulations']} simulation(s) run\"\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\"error\": f\"Unexpected error during simulation: {str(e)}\"},\n            )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        update_simulation_job(\n            task_id,\n            status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n            results={\"error\": f\"Task failed: {str(e)}\"},\n        )",

        "contents": "import os\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nimport traceback\nimport threading\nimport logging\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any\n\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator\n\nfrom db import (\n    create_simulation,\n    get_simulation,\n    get_all_simulations,\n    update_simulation,\n    delete_simulation,\n    get_persona,\n    get_scenario,\n    get_metric,\n    add_persona_to_simulation,\n    add_scenario_to_simulation,\n    add_metric_to_simulation,\n    remove_persona_from_simulation,\n    remove_scenario_from_simulation,\n    remove_metric_from_simulation,\n    get_personas_for_simulation,\n    get_scenarios_for_simulation,\n    get_metrics_for_simulation,\n    get_agent,\n    get_tools_for_agent,\n    create_simulation_job,\n    get_simulation_job,\n    update_simulation_job,\n    get_simulation_jobs_for_simulation,\n)\nfrom utils import (\n    TaskStatus,\n    TaskCreateResponse,\n    get_s3_client,\n    get_s3_output_config,\n)\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/simulations\", tags=[\"simulations\"])\n\n\nclass SimulationCreate(BaseModel):\n    name: str\n    agent_uuid: Optional[str] = None\n    persona_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None\n    scenario_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None\n    metric_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None",

        "originalContents": "import os\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nimport traceback\nimport threading\nimport logging\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any\n\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator\n\nfrom db import (\n    create_simulation,\n    get_simulation,\n    get_all_simulations,\n    update_simulation,\n    delete_simulation,\n    get_persona,\n    get_scenario,\n    get_metric,\n    add_persona_to_simulation,\n    add_scenario_to_simulation,\n    add_metric_to_simulation,\n    remove_persona_from_simulation,\n    remove_scenario_from_simulation,\n    remove_metric_from_simulation,\n    get_personas_for_simulation,\n    get_scenarios_for_simulation,\n    get_metrics_for_simulation,\n    get_agent,\n    get_tools_for_agent,\n    create_simulation_job,\n    get_simulation_job,\n    update_simulation_job,\n    get_simulation_jobs_for_simulation,\n)\nfrom utils import (\n    TaskStatus,\n    TaskCreateResponse,\n    get_s3_client,\n    get_s3_output_config,\n)\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/simulations\", tags=[\"simulations\"])\n\n\nclass SimulationCreate(BaseModel):\n    name: str\n    agent_uuid: Optional[str] = None\n    persona_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None\n    scenario_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None\n    metric_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 63
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/simulations.py"

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Simulations Functions ============\n\n\ndef create_simulation(name: str, agent_id: Optional[str] = None) -\u003e str:\n    \"\"\"Create a new simulation and return its UUID.\"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        simulation_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO simulations (uuid, name, agent_id)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (simulation_uuid, name, agent_id),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(f\"Created simulation with UUID: {simulation_uuid}\")\n        return simulation_uuid",
        "detailedLines": [

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Simulations Functions ============\n\n\ndef create_simulation(name: str, agent_id: Optional[str] = None) -\u003e str:\n    \"\"\"Create a new simulation and return its UUID.\"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        simulation_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO simulations (uuid, name, agent_id)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (simulation_uuid, name, agent_id),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(f\"Created simulation with UUID: {simulation_uuid}\")\n        return simulation_uuid",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1136
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1088
          }

    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{simulation_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a simulation with personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM simulations command\n    with the agent's config and the simulation's personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Uses the agent linked to the simulation and its LLM model configuration.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Simulation not found\")\n\n    # Get agent from simulation\n    agent_uuid = simulation.get(\"agent_id\")\n    if not agent_uuid:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"No agent linked to this simulation. Link an agent to the simulation first.\",\n        )\n\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Get linked entities\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no personas. Add at least one persona.\",\n        )\n\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no scenarios. Add at least one scenario.\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 727,
            "text": "def run_simulation_task("
          },

            "isSignature": true,

          },

            "isSignature": true,

            "text": "    agent: Dict[str, Any],"

            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 730,
            "text": "    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],"
          },

            "text": "    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,

            "text": "    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],"
          },

            "isSignature": true,

            "text": "):"

            "text": "    \"\"\""

            "text": "    Run a simulation with personas, scenarios, and metrics."
```
