          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{simulation_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a simulation with personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM simulations command\n    with the agent's config and the simulation's personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Uses the agent linked to the simulation and its LLM model configuration.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Simulation not found\")\n\n    # Get agent from simulation\n    agent_uuid = simulation.get(\"agent_id\")\n    if not agent_uuid:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"No agent linked to this simulation. Link an agent to the simulation first.\",\n        )\n\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Get linked entities\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no personas. Add at least one persona.\",\n        )\n\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no scenarios. Add at least one scenario.\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 880
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 726
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/simulations.py"
      },
      "score": 0.3964292109012604
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"\", response_model=SimulationCreateResponse)\nasync def create_simulation_endpoint(simulation: SimulationCreate):\n    \"\"\"Create a new simulation with optional linked agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists if provided\n    if simulation.agent_uuid:\n        agent = get_agent(simulation.agent_uuid)\n        if not agent:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Verify all personas exist\n    if simulation.persona_uuids:\n        for persona_uuid in simulation.persona_uuids:\n            persona = get_persona(persona_uuid)\n            if not persona:\n                raise HTTPException(\n                    status_code=404, detail=f\"Persona {persona_uuid} not found\"\n                )\n\n    # Verify all scenarios exist\n    if simulation.scenario_uuids:\n        for scenario_uuid in simulation.scenario_uuids:\n            scenario = get_scenario(scenario_uuid)\n            if not scenario:\n                raise HTTPException(\n                    status_code=404, detail=f\"Scenario {scenario_uuid} not found\"\n                )\n\n    # Verify all metrics exist\n    if simulation.metric_uuids:\n        for metric_uuid in simulation.metric_uuids:\n            metric = get_metric(metric_uuid)\n            if not metric:\n                raise HTTPException(\n                    status_code=404, detail=f\"Metric {metric_uuid} not found\"\n                )\n\n    # Create the simulation\n    simulation_uuid = create_simulation(\n        name=simulation.name, agent_id=simulation.agent_uuid\n    )\n\n    # Add personas to simulation\n    if simulation.persona_uuids:\n        for persona_uuid in simulation.persona_uuids:\n            add_persona_to_simulation(simulation_uuid, persona_uuid)\n\n    # Add scenarios to simulation\n    if simulation.scenario_uuids:\n        for scenario_uuid in simulation.scenario_uuids:\n            add_scenario_to_simulation(simulation_uuid, scenario_uuid)",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"\", response_model=SimulationCreateResponse)\nasync def create_simulation_endpoint(simulation: SimulationCreate):\n    \"\"\"Create a new simulation with optional linked agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists if provided\n    if simulation.agent_uuid:\n        agent = get_agent(simulation.agent_uuid)\n        if not agent:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Verify all personas exist\n    if simulation.persona_uuids:\n        for persona_uuid in simulation.persona_uuids:\n            persona = get_persona(persona_uuid)\n            if not persona:\n                raise HTTPException(\n                    status_code=404, detail=f\"Persona {persona_uuid} not found\"\n                )\n\n    # Verify all scenarios exist\n    if simulation.scenario_uuids:\n        for scenario_uuid in simulation.scenario_uuids:\n            scenario = get_scenario(scenario_uuid)\n            if not scenario:\n                raise HTTPException(\n                    status_code=404, detail=f\"Scenario {scenario_uuid} not found\"\n                )\n\n    # Verify all metrics exist\n    if simulation.metric_uuids:\n        for metric_uuid in simulation.metric_uuids:\n            metric = get_metric(metric_uuid)\n            if not metric:\n                raise HTTPException(\n                    status_code=404, detail=f\"Metric {metric_uuid} not found\"\n                )\n\n    # Create the simulation\n    simulation_uuid = create_simulation(\n        name=simulation.name, agent_id=simulation.agent_uuid\n    )\n\n    # Add personas to simulation\n    if simulation.persona_uuids:\n        for persona_uuid in simulation.persona_uuids:\n            add_persona_to_simulation(simulation_uuid, persona_uuid)\n\n    # Add scenarios to simulation\n    if simulation.scenario_uuids:\n        for scenario_uuid in simulation.scenario_uuids:\n            add_scenario_to_simulation(simulation_uuid, scenario_uuid)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Simulation Jobs Functions ============\n\n\ndef create_simulation_job(\n    simulation_id: str,\n    job_type: str,\n    status: str = \"in_progress\",\n    details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n    results: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n) -\u003e str:\n    \"\"\"Create a new simulation job and return its UUID.\n\n    Args:\n        simulation_id: UUID of the simulation this job is for\n        job_type: Type of job (llm-simulation)\n        status: Initial status (defaults to 'in_progress')\n        details: JSON config needed to re-trigger the job if interrupted\n        results: Initial results (usually None)\n    \"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        job_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n        details_json = json.dumps(details) if details is not None else None\n        results_json = json.dumps(results) if results is not None else None\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO simulation_jobs (uuid, simulation_id, type, status, details, results)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (job_uuid, simulation_id, job_type, status, details_json, results_json),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Created simulation job with UUID: {job_uuid}, type: {job_type}, simulation: {simulation_id}\"\n        )\n        return job_uuid",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Simulation Jobs Functions ============\n\n\ndef create_simulation_job(\n    simulation_id: str,\n    job_type: str,\n    status: str = \"in_progress\",\n    details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n    results: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n) -\u003e str:\n    \"\"\"Create a new simulation job and return its UUID.\n\n    Args:\n        simulation_id: UUID of the simulation this job is for\n        job_type: Type of job (llm-simulation)\n        status: Initial status (defaults to 'in_progress')\n        details: JSON config needed to re-trigger the job if interrupted\n        results: Initial results (usually None)\n    \"\"\"\n    with get_db_connection() as conn:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n        job_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n        details_json = json.dumps(details) if details is not None else None\n        results_json = json.dumps(results) if results is not None else None\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO simulation_jobs (uuid, simulation_id, type, status, details, results)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (job_uuid, simulation_id, job_type, status, details_json, results_json),\n        )\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Created simulation job with UUID: {job_uuid}, type: {job_type}, simulation: {simulation_id}\"\n        )\n        return job_uuid",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1957
          },
          "startPosition": {

          }

    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n\n\n    ...\n\n\n    # Build evaluation criteria from metrics\n    evaluation_criteria = [\n        {\n            \"name\": metric.get(\"name\"),\n            \"description\": metric.get(\"description\") or metric.get(\"name\"),\n        }\n        for metric in metrics\n        if metric.get(\"name\")\n    ]\n\n    return {\n        \"params\": {\"model\": model},\n        \"agent_system_prompt\": agent_config.get(\"system_prompt\", \"\"),\n        \"tools\": tool_configs,\n        \"personas\": persona_list,\n        \"scenarios\": scenario_list,\n        \"evaluation_criteria\": evaluation_criteria,\n    }",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 485,
            "text": "# ============ Run Simulation API ============"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 486
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 487
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 488,
            "text": "def _build_pense_simulation_config("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 489,
            "text": "    agent: Dict[str, Any],"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 490,
            "text": "    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],"

            "text": "    "

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n    # Build evaluation criteria from metrics\n    evaluation_criteria = [\n        {\n            \"name\": metric.get(\"name\"),\n            \"description\": metric.get(\"description\") or metric.get(\"name\"),\n        }\n        for metric in metrics\n        if metric.get(\"name\")\n    ]\n\n    return {\n        \"params\": {\"model\": model},\n        \"agent_system_prompt\": agent_config.get(\"system_prompt\", \"\"),\n        \"tools\": tool_configs,\n        \"personas\": persona_list,\n        \"scenarios\": scenario_list,\n        \"evaluation_criteria\": evaluation_criteria,\n    }",

        "contents": "def init_db():\n\n\n        ...\n\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS scenarios (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                description TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS metrics (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                description TEXT,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS simulations (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                agent_id TEXT DEFAULT NULL,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL,\n                FOREIGN KEY (agent_id) REFERENCES agents(uuid)\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )",

        "originalContents": "\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS scenarios (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                description TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS metrics (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                description TEXT,\n                config TEXT,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )\n\n        cursor.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS simulations (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n                uuid TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n                name TEXT NOT NULL,\n                agent_id TEXT DEFAULT NULL,\n                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n                deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL,\n                FOREIGN KEY (agent_id) REFERENCES agents(uuid)\n            )\n        \"\"\"\n        )",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the LLM simulation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running simulation task {task_id} for agent {agent['uuid']} \"\n            f\"with {len(personas)} persona(s), {len(scenarios)} scenario(s), \"\n            f\"and {len(metrics)} metric(s)\"\n        )\n        update_simulation_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        # Create persistent output directory (not deleted after job completes)\n        output_base_dir = Path(\"simulation_outputs\").resolve()\n        output_base_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        temp_path = output_base_dir / task_id\n        temp_path.mkdir(exist_ok=True)",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the LLM simulation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running simulation task {task_id} for agent {agent['uuid']} \"\n            f\"with {len(personas)} persona(s), {len(scenarios)} scenario(s), \"\n            f\"and {len(metrics)} metric(s)\"\n        )\n        update_simulation_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        # Create persistent output directory (not deleted after job completes)\n        output_base_dir = Path(\"simulation_outputs\").resolve()\n        output_base_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        temp_path = output_base_dir / task_id\n        temp_path.mkdir(exist_ok=True)",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 803
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 558
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/simulations.py"
      },
      "score": 0.3610574007034302
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "{\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"instructions for the bot\",\n  \"tools\": [...], // tool schemas available to the agent LLM\n  \"personas\": [\n    // array of persona objects for users in the simulation\n    {\n      \"characteristics\": \"description of user personality, background, and behavior\",\n      \"gender\": \"female\", // gender of the persona (used in user system prompt)\n      \"language\": \"english\" // language for both agent and user in the simulation\n    }\n  ],\n  \"scenarios\": [...], // different simulation scenarios to be run with each user persona\n  \"evaluation_criteria\": [\n    // array of criteria to evaluate each simulation against\n    {\n      \"name\": \"question_completeness\", // unique name for the criterion\n      \"description\": \"Whether all the questions in the form were covered in the conversation\" // description used by LLM judge to evaluate\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"assistant_behavior\",\n      \"description\": \"Whether the assistant asks one concise question per turn; is empathetic and does not repeat the user's answer back to them.\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nEach simulation pairs every persona with every scenario. The runner fans out into separate folders named &#96;simulation_persona_\u003cn\u003e_scenario_\u003cm\u003e&#96; and saves transcripts, evaluation results, plus logs for inspection. Use the sample persona/scenario set from the example config or replace them with your own scripted behaviors (e.g., \"refuses to share phone number until probed twice\"). Use these artifacts to verify that the agent follows your instructions (e.g., responds appropriately, calls the right tools at the right times, and terminates the call correctly), then iterate on the prompts/config as needed.\n\nAfter each simulation, an LLM judge evaluates the transcript against all &#96;evaluation_criteria&#96; defined in the config. Each criterion produces a match (true/false) and reasoning. The results are aggregated across all simulations at the end of the run.\n\nSet the required environment variables:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 616,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 617,
            "text": "{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 618,
            "text": "  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"instructions for the bot\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 619,
            "text": "  \"tools\": [...], // tool schemas available to the agent LLM"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 620,
            "text": "  \"personas\": ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 621,
            "text": "    // array of persona objects for users in the simulation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 622,
            "text": "    {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 623,
            "text": "      \"characteristics\": \"description of user personality, background, and behavior\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 624,
            "text": "      \"gender\": \"female\", // gender of the persona (used in user system prompt)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 625,
            "text": "      \"language\": \"english\" // language for both agent and user in the simulation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 626,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 627,
            "text": "  ],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 628,
            "text": "  \"scenarios\": [...], // different simulation scenarios to be run with each user persona"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 629,
            "text": "  \"evaluation_criteria\": ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 630,
            "text": "    // array of criteria to evaluate each simulation against"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 631,
            "text": "    {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 632,
            "text": "      \"name\": \"question_completeness\", // unique name for the criterion"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 633,
            "text": "      \"description\": \"Whether all the questions in the form were covered in the conversation\" // description used by LLM judge to evaluate"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 634,
            "text": "    },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 635,
            "text": "    {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 636,
            "text": "      \"name\": \"assistant_behavior\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 637,
            "text": "      \"description\": \"Whether the assistant asks one concise question per turn; is empathetic and does not repeat the user's answer back to them.\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 638,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 639,
            "text": "  ]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 640,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 641,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 642
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 643,
            "text": "Each simulation pairs every persona with every scenario. The runner fans out into separate folders named &#96;simulation_persona_\u003cn\u003e_scenario_\u003cm\u003e&#96; and saves transcripts, evaluation results, plus logs for inspection. Use the sample persona/scenario set from the example config or replace them with your own scripted behaviors (e.g., \"refuses to share phone number until probed twice\"). Use these artifacts to verify that the agent follows your instructions (e.g., responds appropriately, calls the right tools at the right times, and terminates the call correctly), then iterate on the prompts/config as needed."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 644
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 645,
            "text": "After each simulation, an LLM judge evaluates the transcript against all &#96;evaluation_criteria&#96; defined in the config. Each criterion produces a match (true/false) and reasoning. The results are aggregated across all simulations at the end of the run."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 646
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 647,
            "text": "Set the required environment variables:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 648
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 649,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 650,
            "text": "export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 651,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 652
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 653,
            "text": "Then launch simulations:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 654
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 655,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "{\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"instructions for the bot\",\n  \"tools\": [...], // tool schemas available to the agent LLM\n  \"personas\": [\n    // array of persona objects for users in the simulation\n    {\n      \"characteristics\": \"description of user personality, background, and behavior\",\n      \"gender\": \"female\", // gender of the persona (used in user system prompt)\n      \"language\": \"english\" // language for both agent and user in the simulation\n    }\n  ],\n  \"scenarios\": [...], // different simulation scenarios to be run with each user persona\n  \"evaluation_criteria\": [\n    // array of criteria to evaluate each simulation against\n    {\n      \"name\": \"question_completeness\", // unique name for the criterion\n      \"description\": \"Whether all the questions in the form were covered in the conversation\" // description used by LLM judge to evaluate\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"assistant_behavior\",\n      \"description\": \"Whether the assistant asks one concise question per turn; is empathetic and does not repeat the user's answer back to them.\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nEach simulation pairs every persona with every scenario. The runner fans out into separate folders named &#96;simulation_persona_\u003cn\u003e_scenario_\u003cm\u003e&#96; and saves transcripts, evaluation results, plus logs for inspection. Use the sample persona/scenario set from the example config or replace them with your own scripted behaviors (e.g., \"refuses to share phone number until probed twice\"). Use these artifacts to verify that the agent follows your instructions (e.g., responds appropriately, calls the right tools at the right times, and terminates the call correctly), then iterate on the prompts/config as needed.\n\nAfter each simulation, an LLM judge evaluates the transcript against all &#96;evaluation_criteria&#96; defined in the config. Each criterion produces a match (true/false) and reasoning. The results are aggregated across all simulations at the end of the run.\n\nSet the required environment variables:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n",

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


Implementation looks good. Summary of changes:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. **Updated `SimulationCaseResult` Model**
   - Changed `persona` from `str` to `Optional[Dict[str, Any]]` to store the full persona object
   - Changed `scenario` from `str` to `Optional[str]` to store the scenario description

### 2. **Updated Simulation Results Parsing**
   - Added parsing of `config.json` from each simulation directory
   - Extracts `persona` object (with `characteristics`, `gender`, `language`) and `scenario` string from config.json
   - Falls back to extracting indices from directory name if config.json doesn't exist

### 3. **Response Structure**
   Now the simulation results return:
   ```json
   {
     "simulation_results": [
       {
         "simulation_name": "simulation_persona_1_scenario_1",
         "persona": {
           "characteristics": "You are a mother; name is Geeta Prasad...",
           "gender": "female",
           "language": "hindi"
         },
         "scenario": "answer all the questions and complete the form...",
         "evaluation_results": [...],
         "transcript": [...]
       }
     ]
   }
   ```

### How It Works

1. When pense runs simulations, it creates `config.json` in each simulation directory with persona and scenario data (as documented in pense.md lines 740-751)
2. Our code reads this `config.json` file and extracts the full persona object and scenario description
3. Returns the actual persona/scenario data instead of generic labels like "persona_1" or "scenario_1"

The implementation reads from pense-generated config.json files, so no additional storage is needed during simulation creation. The pense tool already stores this data in the output directories.

All changes are complete and linting passes.

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


## Summary of Changes

### 1. **Updated `_build_pense_simulation_config` Function**
   - Added `"label"` field to persona objects (stores persona name)
   - Changed scenarios from strings to objects with `"name"` and `"description"` fields
   - Now generates config that matches the pense.md documentation format

### 2. **Updated `SimulationCaseResult` Model**
   - Changed `scenario` from `Optional[str]` to `Optional[Dict[str, Any]]` to return the full scenario object

### 3. **Updated Config Parsing Logic**
   - Handles scenario as either a string (backward compatibility) or a dict
   - Converts string scenarios to dicts with `{"description": ...}`
   - Updated fallback logic to return scenario as a dict

### Generated Config Structure

Now when creating simulations, the pense config will include:

```json
{
  "personas": [
    {
      "label": "Persona Name",  // NEW: persona name/label
      "characteristics": "...",
      "gender": "female",
      "language": "english"
    }
  ],
  "scenarios": [
    {
      "name": "Scenario Name",  // NEW: scenario name/label
      "description": "scenario description"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Response Structure

The simulation results now return:

```json
{
  "simulation_results": [
    {
      "simulation_name": "simulation_persona_1_scenario_1",
      "persona": {
        "label": "Persona Name",
        "characteristics": "...",
        "gender": "female",
        "language": "hindi"
      },
      "scenario": {
        "name": "Scenario Name",
        "description": "scenario description"
      },
      "evaluation_results": [...],
      "transcript": [...]
    }
  ]
}
```

The code now stores persona labels and scenario objects (with name and description) in the config, and returns full dicts for both persona and scenario in the API response.

---

## Summary of Changes

### 1. **Updated Response Models**
   - Added `name: str` field to `SimulationRunStatusResponse` (format: "Run {index}")
   - Added `name: str` field to `SimulationRunListItem` (format: "Run {index}")

### 2. **Updated `/simulations/run/{task_id}` Endpoint**
   - Calculates the run index by:
     - Getting all jobs for the simulation
     - Sorting by `created_at` ASC (oldest first)
     - Finding the position of the current job (1-indexed)
   - Returns `name` in the format "Run {index}" (e.g., "Run 1", "Run 5")

### 3. **Updated `/simulations/{simulation_uuid}/runs` Endpoint**
   - Calculates run index for each run
   - Sorts jobs by `created_at` ASC (oldest first)
   - Assigns index starting from 1 for each run
   - Returns `name` for each run in the format "Run {index}"

### API Response Examples

**GET `/simulations/run/{task_id}`:**
```json
{
  "task_id": "job-uuid-123",
  "name": "Run 5",
  "status": "done",
  "type": "chat",
  "updated_at": "2024-01-15 10:30:45",
  ...
}
```

**GET `/simulations/{simulation_uuid}/runs`:**
```json
{
  "runs": [
    {
      "uuid": "job-uuid-1",
      "name": "Run 1",
      "status": "done",
      "type": "chat",
      "updated_at": "2024-01-15 10:00:00"
    },
    {
      "uuid": "job-uuid-2",
      "name": "Run 2",
      "status": "done",
      "type": "voice",
      "updated_at": "2024-01-15 11:00:00"
    },
    {
      "uuid": "job-uuid-5",
      "name": "Run 5",
      "status": "in_progress",
      "type": "chat",
      "updated_at": "2024-01-15 14:00:00"
    }
  ]
}
```

The run index is based on creation order: the oldest run is "Run 1", and subsequent runs are numbered sequentially.

_**Agent (composer-1)**_

Updated the `/runs` API to return runs sorted by `updated_at` descending (most recently updated first).

## Summary of Changes

### Updated `/simulations/{simulation_uuid}/runs` Endpoint

1. Calculate run index: sort by `created_at` ASC to determine the index (oldest = "Run 1").
2. Create index mapping: map each job UUID to its run index.
3. Sort for response: sort by `updated_at` DESC (most recently updated first).
4. Assign names: use the mapping to assign the correct run index to each job.

### Result

- Run index is based on creation order (oldest run = "Run 1").
- Response is sorted by `updated_at` DESC (most recently updated first).

### Example Response

```json
{
  "runs": [
    {
      "uuid": "job-uuid-5",
      "name": "Run 5",
      "status": "in_progress",
      "type": "chat",
      "updated_at": "2024-01-15 14:00:00"  // Most recent
    },
    {
      "uuid": "job-uuid-2",
      "name": "Run 2",
      "status": "done",
      "type": "voice",
      "updated_at": "2024-01-15 11:00:00"
    },
    {
      "uuid": "job-uuid-1",
      "name": "Run 1",
      "status": "done",
      "type": "chat",
      "updated_at": "2024-01-15 10:00:00"  // Oldest
    }
  ]
}
```

The runs are now returned sorted by `updated_at` descending, while maintaining correct run index names based on creation order.
